source: Multiple Forza Horizon 6 guides: ForzaFire Platform & Handling, Drivetrain, Community Forums (Forza.net), SkyCoach, MMOExp, FH6 WillGameGuide, calculators.games
reliability: High for concrete numeric ranges and rules; medium for formulas (some sources provide equations, others only empirical ranges). All formulas quoted verbatim from sources. Main caveat: No official Forza formulas exist—all math is community-standard, validated through testing.

categories.formula: No mathematical formula provided. Pressure adjustment by compound: rally/low-spec 26–28 PSI, semi-slicks/slicks/drift ~32 PSI. Traditional baseline for grip: 1.0 Front / 65.0 Rear (from SkyCoach). High Performance/Race/Prototype/GP cars add ~0.5 PSI.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/drag/drift use ~32 PSI. Rally/street/stock use 26–28 PSI. No formulas vary by goal—compound and class determine range.
categories.concreteNumbers: Rally/low-spec: 26–28 PSI. Semi-slicks/slicks/drift: ~32 PSI. Traditional grip baseline: 1.0 Front, 65.0 Rear. HP/Race/Prototype/GP: +0.5 PSI over baseline.

categories.formula: Formula 1 (6-speed standard): FD = (400 - Car HP) ÷ 600 + 4.25. Formula 2 (custom): FD = (Stock HP - Current HP) ÷ 600 + Stock FD. Formula 3 (gear spacing): Rn = A × n^B where A = first gear, B = -0.65 (example: A=3.00 yields 3.00, 1.91, 1.47, 1.22, 1.05, 0.94). Formula 4 (weight-based): FD = [(Weight÷HP) × (Default1st - DefaultLast) ÷ 100] + Default FD. Top speed rule: set top gear to extend 10–20 mph beyond highest lap speed; engine should just reach rev limiter in top gear at longest straight.
categories.goalVariation: All formulas apply across goals. Top speed (drag): maximize FD for lower final ratio. Circuit: balance for power band retention. Gear spacing: wider gaps bottom (control wheelspin), tighter top (wind resistance).
categories.concreteNumbers: Example 325 hp, 6-speed std: 4.38 FD. Power series example: A=3.00, B=-0.65 yields 3.00, 1.91, 1.47, 1.22, 1.05, 0.94. Weight-power example: 2952 lbs, 481 hp = 3.37 FD.

categories.formula: No universal formulas. Camber (all negative): Road asphalt Front -1.0° to -2.0°, Rear -0.5° to -1.0°. Dirt rally Front -0.8° to -1.2°, Rear -0.5° to -0.8°. Cross country Front -0.5°, Rear -0.5°. Drift Front -3.0° to -5.0°, Rear -1.0°. Toe: 0.0° neutral; front toe-out -0.1° to -0.2° for turn-in, rear toe-in +0.1° to +0.3° for stability. Caster: 5.0°–7.0° range; light/agile 5.0–5.5°, mid-weight 5.5–6.5°, heavy/high-speed 6.5–7.0°. RWD requires more front camber; FWD needs more rear camber.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (asphalt): -1.0 to -2.0 front, -0.5 to -1.0 rear. Drift: -3.0 to -5.0 front, -1.0 rear (aggressive). Rally/dirt: -0.8 to -1.2 front, -0.5 to -0.8 rear. Off-road/cross country: -0.5 both. Caster increases with vehicle weight/speed class.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road asphalt: -1.0 to -2.0 front, -0.5 to -1.0 rear. Drift: -3.0 to -5.0 front, -1.0 rear. Caster: 5.0–7.0°; example 7° mentioned. Toe front: -0.1 to -0.2°, rear: +0.1 to +0.3°.

categories.formula: Base ARB = (Weight in lb ÷ 2) ÷ (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Front/Rear distribution by drivetrain: RWD +1 ARB per 1% front-weight deviation from 50%, FWD -1 ARB per 1%, AWD +0.66 ARB per 1%. Stiffness% by class: Sports 61–65%, High Performance 40–46%, Race Car 35–62%. Mechanical balance target: 0.55–0.65 window, sweet spot 0.60.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit uses full formula. Drift/drag: adjust stiffness% and front/rear split for launch/exit traction. Off-road: lower stiffness%. General rule: softer front increases oversteer, softer rear increases understeer.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD typical: Front 18–25, Rear 25–35. AWD typical: Front 22–30, Rear 28–38. FWD typical: Front 8–15, Rear 25–40. Stiffness%: Sports 61–65, HP 40–46, Race 35–62. Mechanical balance target: 0.55–0.65, sweet spot 0.60. Adjustment: ±1 ARB per 1% weight shift (RWD), ±0.66 (AWD), -1 (FWD).

categories.formula: Formula 1 (interpolation): Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Slider Position %) + Min Rate. Formula 2 (frequency-based, imperial): Weight × Weight Distribution % × Multiplier (0.05=0.99Hz soft, 0.10=1.21Hz, 0.20=1.98Hz, 0.30=2.42Hz, 0.40=2.80Hz, 0.50=3.13Hz, 0.60+=3.43Hz+ heavy aero). Frequency Hz = sqrt(K ÷ M × 19.56), K=spring rate, M=weight over axle. Formula 3 (interpolation method): (Max - Min) × Weight Ratio + Min. Weight-based adjustment: 4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50 balance (or 15 lb/in equivalent). Physical constraint: (Front Rate × Ride Height × 2) + (Rear Rate × Ride Height × 2) ≥ vehicle weight.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/road race: target 1.8–2.4 Hz (moderate-to-stiff). Drift: 1.5–2.0 Hz (softer, ~50% front / 85% rear slider positions). Off-road: very soft, ~39–40% front / 6–7% rear. Rally: half race spring values. Baseline percentages: racing Front 70% / Rear 65%, drift Front 50% / Rear 85%, off-road Front 40% / Rear 35%.
categories.concreteNumbers: Baseline slider positions (racing): Front 70%, Rear 65%. Drift: Front 50%, Rear 85%. Off-road: Front 40%, Rear 35%. Weight adjustment: 4 kgf/mm per 1% WD shift or 15 lb/in. Frequency targets: 0.99 Hz (very soft, multiplier 0.05) to 3.43+ Hz (stiff aero, multiplier 0.60+). Constraint: (Front Rate × Height × 2) + (Rear Rate × Height × 2) ≥ weight.

categories.formula: No mathematical formula. Qualitative guidance: Start at the lower point of the range, then raise only if serious bottoming/clearance issues occur. Specific slider range recommendations by type: Race Cars 4–6, Street Cars 5–7, Prototypes 3.5–4.5, Off-roaders 5–7. Discipline guidance: Road Race min both ends, Drag min front/slightly higher rear (rake forward), Drift min both ends, Rally 70–80% of slider range, Cross Country max both ends.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/road race/drift: minimum height both ends. Drag: minimum front, rake rear (higher rear for weight transfer). Rally/off-road: 70–80% slider or maximum range for clearance. Prototypes: lowest 3.5–4.5 range.
categories.concreteNumbers: Race Cars: 4–6. Street Cars: 5–7. Prototypes: 3.5–4.5. Off-roaders: 5–7. Rally: 70–80% of slider. Cross Country: maximum both ends.

categories.formula: Universal rule: Bump = 40–70% of Rebound, target ~60% baseline. Front Bump = Min Bump (by class) + (Front Weight in lb ÷ 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump ÷ 0.6. Rear offset based on spring rate difference: 0–1.5% diff = Rebound -0.2, Bump -0.1; 1.5–35% = Rebound -0.3, Bump -0.2; 36–40% = Rebound -0.6, Bump -0.4; >40% = Rebound -1.2, Bump -0.8. Rally/Off-road add +1.0 rebound both ends. Min Bump by class: Sports 4.4–4.8, High Performance 4.5–4.9, Race Car 4.7–4.9.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing baseline: empirical table shows Circuit 5.0/4.0 (F/R bump), 8.0/7.0 (F/R rebound). Street racing softer: 4.0/3.5 bump, 6.0/5.5 rebound. Drift softer: 2.0/1.0 bump, 4.0/3.0 rebound. Off-road: 1.0/1.0 bump, 3.0/3.0 rebound. Rally: add +1.0 to rebound.
categories.concreteNumbers: Bump rule: 40–70% of rebound, target 60%. Circuit: F 5.0 bump / 8.0 rebound, R 4.0 / 7.0. Street: F 4.0 / 6.0, R 3.5 / 5.5. Drift: F 2.0 / 4.0, R 1.0 / 3.0. Off-road: F 1.0 / 3.0, R 1.0 / 3.0. Min Bump: Sports 4.4–4.8, HP 4.5–4.9, Race 4.7–4.9. Spring diff offsets: 0–1.5% (-0.2R, -0.1B) to >40% (-1.2R, -0.8B).

categories.formula: No mathematical formula. General principle: more downforce = more grip; less downforce = more speed. Aero balance ratio target: 0.4–0.45. Discipline guidance: Time attack uses near-maximum downforce both ends. Sprint uses considerably less. Rule of thumb: front = as much downforce as possible, rear = as little as possible for maximum speed (inverse trade-off).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/time attack: near-maximum downforce, balance 0.4–0.45. Drag/top speed: minimize rear aero for speed (or both low depending on straight length). Drift: balanced aero or lower rear. Rally/off-road: typically low aero. Touge: balance for corner grip vs top-speed straights.
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero balance target: 0.4–0.45. Front = max downforce possible (circuit). Rear = minimized for speed OR maximized for stability (discipline-dependent). Time attack: near-maximum both. Sprint: considerably less than time attack.

categories.formula: Brake Balance (front bias %) by drivetrain: RWD 50–55%, AWD 52–56%, FWD 55–62%, Drift/Dirt 45–50%. Brake Pressure: default 100%, aggressive 105–115%, soft 85–95%. No weight-dependent formulas given.
categories.goalVariation: RWD circuit: 50–55% front bias. AWD circuit: 52–56%. FWD circuit: 55–62% (higher front bias due to weight transfer). Drift/dirt: 45–50% (lower front to avoid lockup). Pressure scales with braking intensity: default 100%, hard braking 105–115%, trail braking 85–95%.
categories.concreteNumbers: Front bias %: RWD 50–55, AWD 52–56, FWD 55–62, Drift/Dirt 45–50. Brake pressure: default 100, aggressive 105–115, soft 85–95.

categories.formula: RWD Acceleration (power-on locking): 40–65% typical, up to 80–100% for drift (near-welded). RWD Deceleration (lift-off locking): 15–30%, exceeding causes lift-off understeer, too low causes oversteer. AWD/FWD follow same logic applied to respective axles. Center Balance (rear torque %): AWD varies by discipline: Road Racing 70–85% rear (never below 65%), Dirt Rally 65–75% rear, Cross Country 55–65% rear. General AWD rule: never below 50% rear bias. Optional aggressive exit-speed formula: up to ~90% accel, center 70–80% rear. Deceleration ranges: 10–15% typical, up to 20–25% for trail-braking.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit RWD: 40–65% accel / 15–30% decel. Circuit AWD: 28% front accel / 0% front decel / 100% rear accel / 45% rear decel / 70–85% center. Drift RWD: 80–100% accel / 15–40% decel. Drag/exit-speed AWD: up to 90% accel, 70–80% center. Rally AWD: 30–40% front accel / 0–15% front decel / 55–70% rear accel / 20–25% rear decel / 65–75% center. Cross Country: 35–45% front accel / 5–15% front decel / 65–75% rear accel / 20–25% rear decel / 55–65% center.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD accel: 40–65% circuit, 80–100% drift. RWD decel: 15–30% circuit, 15–40% drift. Center balance: never below 50%; circuit 70–85%, rally 65–75%, cross country 55–65%. AWD road race example: front accel 28%, rear accel 100%, center 70–85%. Aggressive exit accel up to 90%; decel 10–15% base, 20–25% trail-brake.

reliability: High. The source provides exact numeric formulas, specific PSI ranges, percentage values, and degree measurements. Formulas are explicitly stated (e.g., FD = 4.25 + ((400 − Power) / 6) × 0.01). The guide includes tuning order, discipline-specific baselines, and edge cases with concrete numbers.

categories.formula: Stock/Street/Rally: 26–28 PSI front & rear. Sport: ~28 PSI. Semi/Race Slicks: ~32 PSI. Drift: ~32 PSI (variable low to high). Drag: Max front / Min rear. High-performance cars: +0.5 PSI on top of base. Adjustment increment: 0.5 PSI at a time.
categories.concreteNumbers: Stock 26-28 PSI, Sport 28 PSI, Race/Drift 32 PSI, Drag max/min. Increment 0.5 PSI per adjustment.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Rally: 26-28 PSI. Drift: 32 PSI variable. Drag: maximum front, minimum rear. High-performance baseline: +0.5 PSI.
categories.edgeCases: Drift requires variable low-to-high pressures front/rear. Drag uses extreme min/max. High-performance cars get +0.5 PSI bonus across all baselines.

categories.formula: FD = 4.25 + ((400 − Power) / 6) × 0.01. For 800+ hp: halve power input first. Discipline adjustments: Rally +0.50, Off-road +0.75, Drag −0.20, Drift +0.10. Aero offset: −0.05 to −0.40 depending on aero level.
categories.concreteNumbers: Base formula: FD = 4.25 + ((400 − Power) / 6) × 0.01. Examples: 250 hp = 4.50, 400 hp = 4.25, 600 hp = 3.91. Rally +0.50, Off-road +0.75, Drag −0.20, Drift +0.10. Aero −0.05 to −0.40.
categories.goalVariation: Rally: +0.50 vs road. Off-road: +0.75. Drag: −0.20. Drift: +0.10. Aero level reduces by −0.05 to −0.40 depending on downforce setting.
categories.edgeCases: 800+ hp: halve power before calculation. Aero offset varies with downforce level.

categories.formula: Camber: start −1.0 to −1.5°, adjust in 0.3° increments toward more negative. Maximum useful ~−2.0° (beyond that, adjust caster instead). Caster: baseline 6.5–7.0°; lighter cars can drop to 5–6°. Toe: front toe-out sharpens turn-in; rear toe-in stabilizes RWD on throttle.
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber: −1.0 to −1.5° baseline, 0.3° increment, maximum useful ~−2.0°. Caster: 6.5–7.0° baseline; light cars 5–6°. RWD Drift rear toe: −0.1 to −0.2°.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Race: −1.5 to −2° front/rear. Drift: front −5° max, rear ~−1°. Caster maxed for drift turn-in sharpening.
categories.edgeCases: RWD: slightly more negative front than rear. FWD: slightly more negative rear than front. AWD: balanced. Open-wheel: drop rear camber by ~1.0°. Drift: front −5° (max), rear ~−1°.

categories.formula: RWD baseline: 20 front / 55 rear. Target mechanical balance 0.55–0.65 (sweet spot 0.60). Below 0.55: soften front ARB or stiffen rear ARB. Above 0.65: stiffen front ARB or soften rear ARB. FWD wants stiffer rear (40+). AWD: less front, more rear if understeering. Drift: soft both (~8 if response desired, fully soft otherwise).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: 20 front / 55 rear. FWD rear: 40+. Drift: ~8. Mechanical balance target: 0.55–0.65 (optimum 0.60).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit RWD: 20 front / 55 rear. FWD: stiffer rear (40+). AWD: less front, more rear. Drift: soft (~8 or fully soft).
categories.edgeCases: Stiffer end loses grip first. FWD characteristic requires stiffer rear. AWD adjustment: reduce understeer via less front / more rear. Mechanical balance window 0.55–0.65 universal.

categories.formula: No specific numeric formula given. Philosophy: softer springs absorb bumps and keep tire planted (more body roll); stiffer springs reduce body roll (risk of skating rough surfaces). Ride height: start at minimum for car type, front and rear matched. Drift baseline: soft, front and rear matched (~400 lb/in baseline). Damping relationship: bump should be 40–70% of rebound (most cars happiest around 60%).
categories.concreteNumbers: Ride height: minimum for car type, matched F/R. Drift: ~400 lb/in baseline. Bump/rebound ratio: 40–70% bump (optimum ~60% of rebound).
categories.goalVariation: Drift: soft springs (~400 lb/in), matched F/R. Race/Grip: moderate-stiff, bump 60% of rebound. Rough terrain: softer for tire contact.
categories.edgeCases: Softer = more body roll but better tire contact. Stiffer = less body roll but may skip rough surfaces. Bump/rebound ratio (40–70%, optimum 60%) universal rule.

categories.formula: Universal rule: bump should be 40–70% of rebound, with most cars happiest around 60%. Race/Grip baseline: ~14 / ~14 rebound (F/R), ~8 / ~8 bump (F/R). AWD soft meta: ~18 / ~18 rebound, ~6 / ~6 bump. Drift: ~4 / 4 / 4 / 4 (soft). Drag RWD: Front soft rebound / stiff bump; Rear stiff rebound / soft bump. Effect: softer front rebound = oversteer on entry; softer rear rebound = understeer on entry.
categories.concreteNumbers: Race/Grip: 14 rebound F/R, 8 bump F/R. AWD soft: 18 rebound F/R, 6 bump F/R. Drift: 4/4/4/4. Bump/rebound ratio: 40–70% (optimum 60%). Drag RWD: soft rebound / stiff bump (front), stiff rebound / soft bump (rear).
categories.goalVariation: Race/Grip: 14 rebound, 8 bump baseline. AWD soft meta: 18 rebound, 6 bump. Drift: 4/4/4/4. Drag RWD: asymmetric F/R (soft rebound front, stiff rear).
categories.edgeCases: Softer front rebound causes oversteer on entry. Softer rear rebound causes understeer. Drag RWD requires asymmetric tuning (soft front rebound / stiff bump, opposite rear). Bump/rebound ratio (60% optimal) universal.

categories.formula: New FH6 stat workflow: (1) adjust front and rear downforce until Aero Balance stat reads 0.40–0.45; (2) set overall level by feel; (3) add front downforce to fix high-speed understeer, add rear to fix high-speed oversteer. Drivetrain bias: RWD wants slight rear bias; FWD/AWD want slight front bias.
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance stat target: 0.40–0.45. Front/rear downforce: adjust until target stat reached.
categories.goalVariation: RWD: slight rear bias. FWD/AWD: slight front bias. High-speed understeer: add front downforce. High-speed oversteer: add rear downforce.
categories.edgeCases: FH6-specific stat workflow (Aero Balance 0.40–0.45 target). RWD/FWD/AWD have different bias preferences. Downforce adjustment is directional (front for understeer, rear for oversteer).

categories.formula: Bias starting points: Stable AWD 52–55% front, Aggressive RWD 48–51% front, Touge/Mountain 50–52% front, FWD 55–60% front, Highway/Top Speed 54–58% front, Drift tandem 55–70% (toward front). Pressure baseline: 100% unless lockup issue. Lockup tuning: drop or raise 5–10% for adjustment. Sweet spot: tire lockup in last 10–15% of trigger pull. Adjustment sensitivity: 1% makes noticeable difference.
categories.concreteNumbers: AWD: 52–55%. RWD: 48–51%. Touge: 50–52%. FWD: 55–60%. Highway: 54–58%. Drift tandem: 55–70%. Pressure: 100% baseline. Lockup adjustment: ±5–10%. Sweet spot: lockup at 10–15% trigger pull.
categories.goalVariation: Stable AWD: 52–55%. Aggressive RWD: 48–51%. Touge: 50–52%. FWD: 55–60%. Highway/top speed: 54–58%. Drift tandem: 55–70% (front bias).
categories.edgeCases: FWD has higher front bias (55–60%) vs RWD (48–51%). Drift tandem heavily biased front (55–70%). Pressure typically 100%; adjust ±5–10% only for lockup issues. 1% bias change is noticeable.

categories.formula: RWD Race: 50–60% accel / 10–20% decel (rear). FWD Race: 80–95% accel / 5–10% decel (front). AWD Race: Front 80–95% accel / 5–10% decel; Rear 50–70% accel / 10–15% decel; Center 70–80% rear. AWD Touge: Front 15–25% accel / 5–10% decel; Rear 65–80% accel / 15–20% decel; Center 70–80% rear. RWD Drift: 95–100% accel / 10–40% decel (rear). Rally/Off-road: baseline + 24% A / −20% D both axles; Center 60–70% rear. Open-wheel: accel ~30%, decel 0%. Hard limits: don't push accel above 95% on FWD/AWD, don't drop decel below 5%, AWD center never below 50%, accel increment only 2% (use even numbers).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD Race: 50–60% accel / 10–20% decel. FWD Race: 80–95% accel / 5–10% decel. AWD Race front: 80–95% / 5–10%; rear 50–70% / 10–15%; center 70–80%. AWD Touge front: 15–25% / 5–10%; rear 65–80% / 15–20%; center 70–80%. RWD Drift: 95–100% accel / 10–40% decel. Rally/Off-road: +24% A / −20% D; center 60–70%. Open-wheel: ~30% accel / 0% decel. Increment: 2% (use even numbers).
categories.goalVariation: Race (RWD/FWD/AWD): balanced accel/decel per drivetrain. Touge (AWD): front looser (15–25%), rear grippier (65–80%). Drift (RWD): maximum accel (95–100%). Rally/Off-road: +24% accel, −20% decel.
categories.edgeCases: RWD rear only. FWD/AWD: high accel (80–95%), never above 95%. AWD center: never below 50%. Decel never below 5%. Accel always even numbers (2% increments). RWD Drift: rear 95–100% accel, high decel (10–40%). Rally/Off-road: formula-based (+24%A / −20%D). Open-wheel: ~30% accel / 0% decel.

source: https://www.forzafire.com/guides (Platform & Handling, Drivetrain, Tires & Rims, Aero & Appearance, Engine guides)
reliability: High. The site provides concrete numeric ranges, formulas, and starting points with clear application rules. Some domains (engine) lack detailed formulas due to Forza architecture (no tuning sliders), but this is accurately reported. Tire pressures, damping calculations, and ARB formulas are precise and quantified.

categories.formula: Baseline PSI by compound: Stock/Street 31.0, Sport 31.5, Semi-Slick 32.0, Race Slicks 32.5, Rally 29.0-30.0, Off-Road 28.0-30.0, Drift 20.0-26.0. Front/Rear split: RWD +0.5-1.0 PSI front, FWD +1.0-2.0 PSI front, AWD equal or +0.2-0.5 PSI front. Weight rule: 600kg kei car = 28-30 PSI on sport; 1800kg muscle car = 33-35 PSI (same compound). Tire width +0.5-1.0 PSI per 50mm width increase. Adjustment increments: 0.5 PSI per iteration.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (Race Slicks 31.5-32.5 PSI), Drag (25 PSI rear / 32 PSI front), Drift (20-22 PSI), Rally (29-30 PSI), Cross-Country (28-30 PSI), Off-Road (28-30 PSI)
categories.edgeCases: RWD/FWD/AWD all have distinct front-rear splits. Drift requires unusually low pressure (20-26 PSI) due to low-grip need. FH6 applies higher PI penalty for Race Slicks on dirt, mandating Rally tires for mixed builds.
categories.concreteNumbers: Sport baseline 31.5 PSI; Race Slicks 32.5 PSI; Rally 29-30 PSI; Drift 20-26 PSI. RWD front +0.5-1.0 PSI above rear. FWD front +1.0-2.0 PSI above rear. AWD front +0.2-0.5 PSI above rear. Light cars (600kg) -3-5 PSI vs heavy cars (1800kg). Pressure adjustment: 0.5 PSI per diagnostics pass.

categories.formula: Final Drive slider adjustment conceptual: raising shortens all gears (more acceleration, lower top speed); lowering lengthens all gears (lower acceleration, higher top speed). Race Transmission gear count: 6-7 speeds for road, 8-10 for drag/extreme powerband. Empirical recommendation: adjust and monitor 0-100 km/h time and top speed.
categories.goalVariation: Road racing (6-7 gears, empirical FD tuning), Drag (8-10 gears, favor lower ratios), Rally/Off-Road (similar empirical approach with weight influence noted but unquantified)
categories.edgeCases: Only Race Transmission allows gear count adjustment. Weight effect on optimal FD is noted but no formula provided. Standard transmissions lack tuning sliders.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road: 6-7 gears. Drag: 8-10 gears. No specific Final Drive ratios or multipliers given; adjustment is slider-based with empirical testing.

categories.formula: Camber (degrees) by discipline: Road asphalt front -1.0 to -2.0 / rear -0.5 to -1.0. Dirt rally front -0.8 to -1.2 / rear -0.5 to -0.8. Cross-country front -0.5 / rear -0.5. Drift front -3.0 to -5.0 / rear -1.0. Toe: neutral (0.0) is default; front toe-out -0.1 to -0.2 for sharper turn-in; rear toe-in +0.1 to +0.3 for high-speed stability. Caster by car class: light/agile 5.0-5.5, mid-weight road race 5.5-6.5, heavier/high-speed 6.5-7.0. Warning: caster above 7.0 causes contact patch to switch violently.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road asphalt settings, neutral toe), Drift (extreme front camber -3.0 to -5.0), Rally (mild -0.8 to -1.2), Cross-Country (minimal -0.5 all around), Drag (minimal camber, rake via ride height)
categories.edgeCases: Drift requires far more negative front camber (-3.0 to -5.0) than road racing (-1.0 to -2.0). Caster over 7.0 degrees becomes unstable. Toe-in/out adjustments are discipline-specific (turn-in vs stability priority).
categories.concreteNumbers: Road camber: front -1.0 to -2.0, rear -0.5 to -1.0 deg. Drift camber: front -3.0 to -5.0, rear -1.0 deg. Rally camber: front -0.8 to -1.2, rear -0.5 to -0.8 deg. Cross-country camber: -0.5 all around. Toe turn-in: -0.1 to -0.2 front. Toe stability: +0.1 to +0.3 rear. Caster light 5.0-5.5, mid 5.5-6.5, heavy 6.5-7.0, max safe 7.0 deg.

categories.formula: Base ARB = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Stiffness% varies by class: Sports 61-65%, High Performance 40-46%, Race Car 35-62%. Distribution adjustment per drivetrain: RWD +1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%; FWD -1 ARB per 1%; AWD +0.66 per 1%. Typical ranges by drivetrain: RWD front 18-25 / rear 25-35; AWD front 22-30 / rear 28-38; FWD front 8-15 / rear 25-40.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (race stiff settings within ranges), Drag (minimal rear ARB), Drift (stiff rear, softer front to enable rotation), Rally/Off-Road (moderate values, rear-biased)
categories.edgeCases: FWD naturally has low front ARB (8-15) despite heavy front weighting; instead ARBs are rear-biased (25-40 rear). RWD is front-understeery by nature so +adjustment applied. AWD is middle ground at +0.66 per 1%.
categories.concreteNumbers: Formula: (Weight_lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). RWD adjustment +1.0 per 1% front weight above 50%. FWD adjustment -1.0 per 1%. AWD adjustment +0.66 per 1%. RWD ranges: F18-25, R25-35. AWD ranges: F22-30, R28-38. FWD ranges: F8-15, R25-40. Class stiffness: Sports 61-65%, HP 40-46%, Race 35-62%.

categories.formula: Spring Rate slider positions by class and drivetrain. Sports class: RWD/AWD front 87-98% / rear 58-80%; FWD front 58-80% / rear 87-98%. High Performance: RWD/AWD front 85-93% / rear 63-84%; FWD front 63-84% / rear 85-93%. Race Car: RWD/AWD front 83-93% / rear 59-85%; FWD front 59-85% / rear 83-93%. Weight distribution adjustment: shift roughly 4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50 split. Rally springs: use half the Race spring values. Off-Road springs: front 39-40% / rear 6-7% slider position.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (Race class values), Drag (Race class with slight forward rake via ride height adjustment), Drift (Race class), Rally (half of Race values), Off-Road (39-40% front / 6-7% rear), Cross-Country (maximum both ends)
categories.edgeCases: FWD has reversed spring ranges (soft front, stiff rear) vs RWD/AWD (stiff front, soft rear). Rally uses exactly half of Race values. Off-Road is extreme rear-soft (6-7%). Cross-country is maximum-stiff at both ends.
categories.concreteNumbers: Sports RWD: F87-98%, R58-80%. Sports FWD: F58-80%, R87-98%. HP RWD: F85-93%, R63-84%. HP FWD: F63-84%, R85-93%. Race RWD: F83-93%, R59-85%. Race FWD: F59-85%, R83-93%. Weight adjustment: 4 kgf/mm per 1% from 50/50. Rally = half Race. Off-Road: F39-40%, R6-7%.

categories.formula: Discipline-specific slider targets: Road race minimum both ends. Dirt rally 70-80% of slider range. Cross-country maximum both ends. Drag minimum front, slightly higher rear. Drift minimum both ends.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (minimum), Drag (min front + slightly higher rear for slight rake), Drift (minimum), Rally (70-80%), Cross-Country (maximum)
categories.edgeCases: Drag is the only discipline that uses rake (lower front, higher rear). Rally sits middle at 70-80%. Cross-country needs clearance so maximum.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road race: minimum both ends. Dirt rally: 70-80% of range. Cross-country: maximum both ends. Drag: minimum front, slightly elevated rear. Drift: minimum both ends.

categories.formula: Core rule: Bump = 40-70% of Rebound, target ~60% baseline. Front Bump = Min_Bump + (Front_Weight_lb / 200) × 0.1. Minimum bump by class: Sports 4.4-4.8, High Performance 4.5-4.9, Race Car 4.7-4.9. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6. Rear damping offset (based on spring rate difference): 0-1.5% diff (Rear Rebound -0.2, Rear Bump -0.1), 1.5-35% diff (Rear Rebound -0.3, Rear Bump -0.2), 36-40% diff (Rear Rebound -0.6, Rear Bump -0.4), above 40% diff (Rear Rebound -1.2, Rear Bump -0.8). Rally/Off-Road: add +1.0 to rebound across the board.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (apply base formula), Drag (base formula with potential slight rebound increase), Drift (base formula with potential stiffness adjustment), Rally (add +1.0 to all rebound), Off-Road (add +1.0 to all rebound)
categories.edgeCases: Rally and Off-Road both add +1.0 globally to rebound for compliance. Rear damping requires calculation of spring rate difference and three breakpoints (0-1.5%, 1.5-35%, 36-40%, 40%+). FWD spring stiffness is inverted vs RWD, affecting the differential calculation.
categories.concreteNumbers: Bump = 40-70% Rebound (target 60%). Front Bump = Min + (FrontWt_lb / 200) × 0.1. Sports Min 4.4-4.8, HP Min 4.5-4.9, Race Min 4.7-4.9. Front Rebound = FrontBump / 0.6. Spring diff 0-1.5%: RR-0.2, RB-0.1. 1.5-35%: RR-0.3, RB-0.2. 36-40%: RR-0.6, RB-0.4. >40%: RR-1.2, RB-0.8. Rally/Off-Road: +1.0 Rebound global.

categories.formula: Aero Balance target by discipline: Road Racing AWD 0.40-0.45 (front-biased to fight understeer), Road Racing RWD 0.50-0.55 (neutral/rear-biased), Road Racing FWD 0.45-0.55 (front-biased), Drift 0.45-0.50 (enable rear breakaway), Drag minimize total, Dirt/Cross-Country ~0.50. Efficiency benchmarks: above 0.85 excellent, 0.70-0.85 average, below 0.70 poor. Front downforce by drivetrain: AWD maximum, RWD 60-80% of slider, FWD high to maximum. Rear downforce by drivetrain: AWD minimum rear (counterintuitive), RWD 60-90% of slider, FWD low to moderate. Unit: 1 KGF ≈ 2.2 lb.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit RWD (0.50-0.55 balance), Circuit AWD (0.40-0.45 balance), Circuit FWD (0.45-0.55 balance), Drift (0.45-0.50 balance), Drag (minimize total, balance irrelevant)
categories.edgeCases: AWD requires front-biased aero (0.40-0.45) with minimum rear downforce (counterintuitive finding: soft rear downforce despite rear weight bias in diffs). RWD is neutral to slightly rear-biased. FWD is front-biased. Drag racing aero is minimized; balance is irrelevant.
categories.concreteNumbers: Balance targets: RWD circuit 0.50-0.55, AWD circuit 0.40-0.45, FWD circuit 0.45-0.55, Drift 0.45-0.50, Dirt/CC ~0.50. Efficiency >0.85 excellent, 0.70-0.85 avg, <0.70 poor. Downforce: AWD F=max/R=min, RWD F=60-80%/R=60-90%, FWD F=high-max/R=low-mod. Conversion: 1 KGF = 2.2 lb.

categories.formula: Brake Balance (front-to-rear bias %): RWD 50-55%, AWD 52-56%, FWD 55-62%, Drift/Dirt 45-50%. Brake Pressure: default 100%, aggressive 105-115%, softer 85-95%.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (bias per drivetrain: RWD 50-55%, AWD 52-56%, FWD 55-62%), Drag (not specified; likely circuit-appropriate), Drift (45-50% bias), Rally/Dirt (45-50% bias)
categories.edgeCases: FWD requires highest front bias (55-62%) due to inherent rear-biased weight distribution. RWD uses neutral bias. Drift and dirt off-road both shift to softer rear bias (45-50%) to reduce rear brake lock. Pressure adjustment is global (105-115% for aggression, 85-95% for softness).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD balance: 50-55%. AWD balance: 52-56%. FWD balance: 55-62%. Drift/Dirt balance: 45-50%. Pressure default 100%, aggressive 105-115%, soft 85-95%.

categories.formula: RWD Acceleration (road racing): 40-65% range. RWD Deceleration (road racing): 15-30% range. Warning: too high causes lift-off understeer; too low causes lift-off oversteer. AWD Center Balance (torque split to rear): Road 70-85% rear bias, Dirt 65-75% rear bias, Cross-Country 55-65% mild rear bias. General guideline: 70%+ starting point; longer wheelbase tolerates up to 90%. Common starting points (full diff config): Road racing (Front Accel 28%, Front Decel 0%, Rear Accel 100%, Rear Decel 45%, Center 70-85%), Dirt rally (Front Accel 30-40%, Front Decel 0-15%, Rear Accel 55-70%, Rear Decel 20-25%, Center 65-75%), Cross-Country (Front Accel 35-45%, Front Decel 5-15%, Rear Accel 65-75%, Rear Decel 20-25%, Center 55-65%).
categories.goalVariation: Road racing RWD (Accel 40-65%, Decel 15-30%), Road racing AWD (Front A 28%/D 0%, Rear A 100%/D 45%, Center 70-85%), Drift (RWD Accel 80-100% near-welded), Rally AWD (Center 65-75% rear bias), Cross-Country AWD (Center 55-65% mild rear bias)
categories.edgeCases: RWD has no center balance option (only Accel/Decel). AWD center balance ranges vary dramatically by discipline (70-85% road vs 55-65% cross-country). Longer wheelbase (270+ mm) tolerates higher center balance (up to 90%). Drift is near-welded (80-100% Accel).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD road: Accel 40-65%, Decel 15-30%. RWD drift: Accel 80-100%. AWD road: FA28%, FD0%, RA100%, RD45%, C70-85%. AWD rally: FA30-40%, FD0-15%, RA55-70%, RD20-25%, C65-75%. AWD cross-country: FA35-45%, FD5-15%, RA65-75%, RD20-25%, C55-65%.

reliability: High - comprehensive guide with specific formulas, ranges, and step-by-step tuning procedures. Source is ForzaTune, created by Anthony Curtis with engineering and physics background. Concrete numbers provided throughout.

categories.formula: Set cold tire pressure between 27-30 PSI (1.90-2.07 Bar). Target warm tire pressure between 32-34 PSI (2.20-2.35 Bar). In Motorsport, middle tire temperature should be the target metric — if middle is too low, increase pressure; if too high, decrease pressure. Start with cold pressure 25-35 PSI (1.72-2.41 Bar) in Motorsport.
categories.concreteNumbers: Cold: 27-30 PSI (FH6), 25-35 PSI (Motorsport). Warm/Target: 32-34 PSI (FH6). Temperature range: 170-250°F or 75-120°C optimal.
categories.goalVariation: Street/track: use telemetry-based pressure tuning. High-speed tracks: can use lower compounds due to cooler operation. Technical tracks: upgrade compounds to maintain grip.
categories.edgeCases: FH6/FH5: tire temperatures variable due to weather/water; pick consistent testing circuit. Motorsport: new tire model requires heat evenness across three temperature zones (inner/middle/outer).

categories.formula: Camber: Use default or start -1.0 to -1.5 degrees. Target outer tire temperature equal to middle temp as car exits turn. Adjust camber more negative if outside edge hotter; move toward zero if inside hotter. Do not exceed -2.0 deg front without increasing caster. Toe: Keep at 0 degrees for most setups (FWD exception: 0.1 toe-out if turn-in hesitation; RWD exception: 0.1 toe-in rear if oversteer). Caster: Start 5.5 degrees or default if higher; optimal range 5.5-6.5 degrees. Values above 6.0 may cause snappiness.
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber: -1.0 to -1.5 deg start, max -2.0 deg front. Toe: 0 deg standard; exceptions 0.1 deg only. Caster: 5.5-6.5 deg range.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: balanced camber/caster for consistent grip. Turn-in hesitation (FWD): add 0.1 toe-out front. Oversteer (RWD): add 0.1 toe-in rear. Long straights: increase caster if weaving; decrease if snappy.
categories.edgeCases: FH5: toe settings unpredictable; use zero toe in FH5. FH6 better toe response. Avoid excessive caster (6.0+) on FH6 due to snap. Camber adjustment must follow suspension geometry changes.

categories.formula: Heavier vehicles require stiffer springs. Weight distribution balance: if front weight % decreases, subtract spring rate from front and add to rear. Rule: 15 lb/in (4 kgf/mm) change per 1% weight distribution shift. Example: 2% front weight loss = subtract 30 lb/in front, add 30 lb/in rear. Lower ride height increases bottoming-out risk. Soft springs (avoid bottoming) + not too stiff (avoid skipping) = goal.
categories.concreteNumbers: Spring rate adjustment: 15 lb/in per 1% weight distribution change (4 kgf/mm equivalent). Ride height: low for smooth tracks, higher for bumpy surfaces.
categories.goalVariation: Smooth circuit: lower ride height for CoG advantage; stiffer springs to prevent bottoming. Bumpy/off-road: higher ride height for compression clearance; softer springs. Front weight shift: adjust front springs, mirror adjustment to rear.
categories.edgeCases: AWD/RWD: match front/rear pressures initially. Lowered cars prone to bottoming; must stiffen springs accordingly. Rally suspension not for road tuning (common mistake).

categories.formula: Front weight % change = ARB adjustment rule: for every 1% front weight removed, subtract 0.5 from front ARB and add 0.5 to rear ARB. Rough guidance: balance adjustment to match weight distribution. For understeer: reduce front ARB. For oversteer: reduce rear ARB. Do not compensate rear problem with front adjustment.
categories.concreteNumbers: ARB adjustment: 0.5 point per 1% weight distribution change. No specific stiffness values given (adjusted per balance needs).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: ARB is primary tool for oversteer/understeer tuning. Mid-corner handling primary effect. Tight tracks: higher ARB values for responsiveness. Sweeping curves: moderate ARB for balance.
categories.edgeCases: ARBs have no straight-line effect. Immediate noticeable effect during turns. If ARB >20-25 still lacks responsiveness, increase roll center instead. Match ARB changes to damper changes for consistency.

categories.formula: Damper change rule: for every 1% front weight reduced (from upgrades), subtract 0.2 from front rebound and front bump, and add 0.2 to rear values. Do the opposite if front weight increases. Bump ~66% of rebound (rough): bump usually less than rebound (~two-thirds). Example: 2% front weight loss = 0.2 × 2 = 0.4 point reduction front, 0.4 point addition rear (both rebound and bump).
categories.concreteNumbers: Damper adjustment: 0.2 points per 1% weight distribution change. Bump typically around 66% (two-thirds) of rebound value.
categories.goalVariation: Smooth track: more damping. Bumpy/irregular: less damping. Understeer (turn-in): soften front damping. Oversteer/lift-off throttle: soften rear damping.
categories.edgeCases: Forza does not auto-adjust dampers on weight distribution change. Must manually adjust. Stiffer springs require stiffer dampers to match.

categories.formula: Lower ride height: better CoG, better handling, but increases bottoming-out risk and reduces spring compression room. Higher ride height: more clearance for bumps, less bottoming risk. Check suspension telemetry bar graph — if reaching maximum during driving, increase ride height. If ride height maxed and bottoming still occurs, increase spring rates.
categories.concreteNumbers: No specific inch/mm values given; relative rule: low for smooth tracks, high for rough tracks. Telemetry guideline: suspension bars must not reach maximum compression during normal driving.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/smooth: minimum viable ride height for CoG. Bumpy/off-road: higher ride height for clearance. Bottoming check: increase ride height first; if maxed, increase springs.
categories.edgeCases: Lowered ride height necessitates stiffer springs. Rally suspension for different geometry. Telemetry check is verification step after suspension tuning.

categories.formula: Start with front slider between midpoint and 3/4 of maximum depending on top-speed trade-off desired. Rear aero ~25-50% less than front for balance. In FH6: target aero balance ~0.50 (measured in-game). Aero benefits only at higher speeds; requires fast enough speeds to generate downforce effect.
categories.concreteNumbers: Front aero: midpoint to 3/4 maximum. Rear aero: 25-50% less than front. FH6 target: aero balance ~0.50.
categories.goalVariation: High-speed corners: beneficial for grip and exit speed. Long straights: reduce aero to max top speed (minimize/0 setting). Tight winding tracks: aero less useful (insufficient speed to benefit). Speed-focused: minimum aero.
categories.edgeCases: AWD/RWD: apply aero symmetrically (both front and rear) to avoid speed imbalance. Oversized aero upgrades visually: personal choice if worth tuning. Aero balance rear slide: increase rear downforce. Aero balance front slide: increase front downforce.

categories.formula: Start with brake balance at 50% (default). Brake pressure set to 100%. Adjust balance 1% at a time if tires lock uncontrollably. Trail-braking + rear slide: shift balance toward front. Trail-braking + turn-in understeer: shift balance 1-2% toward rear. Straight-line + front lock: shift rear. Straight-line + rear lock: shift front. Target tire lock in last 10-15% of trigger/pedal movement for resolution.
categories.concreteNumbers: Starting balance: 50%. Starting pressure: 100%. Adjustment: 1% increments. Lock target: last 10-15% of trigger/pedal travel.
categories.goalVariation: Trail-braking heavy: bias toward front (rear slide issue = increase front brake). Straight-line only: balance for even front/rear lock. ABS off: more sensitivity needed; small adjustments 1%. ABS on: defaults often work; fine-tune as needed.
categories.edgeCases: FH5 bug: brake balance backwards (slider right = more front, opposite of Motorsport/FH6). Motorsport/FH6: slider left = more front. Pressure: reduce 5-10% if both wheels lock frequently; increase if brakes weak at mid-travel.

categories.formula: RWD acceleration: 40-60% (Motorsport/FH6), 25-45% (earlier Horizon); tight turns = lower end. RWD deceleration: 20-40% (Motorsport/FH6), 30-45% (earlier Horizon); more understeer = higher end. FWD acceleration: 20-30% (Motorsport/FH6), 15-25% (earlier); high power = lower end. FWD deceleration: 0-10% (Motorsport/FH6), 10-20% (earlier); more understeer = higher end. AWD: use FWD/RWD as starting points; center diff 60-70% range. Adjustment increments: 2-3% for oversteer/understeer changes.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD accel: 40-60% (FM/FH6) or 25-45% (FH5-). RWD decel: 20-40% (FM/FH6) or 30-45% (FH5-). FWD accel: 20-30% (FM/FH6) or 15-25% (FH5-). FWD decel: 0-10% (FM/FH6) or 10-20% (FH5-). AWD center: 60-70%. Adjustments: 1-3% increments.
categories.goalVariation: RWD turn-entry oversteer: increase deceleration 2-3%. RWD turn-exit oversteer: decrease acceleration 2-3%. FWD turn-entry understeer: decrease deceleration 1%. FWD turn-exit understeer: decrease acceleration 2-3%. AWD mid-corner: increase center split for oversteer.
categories.edgeCases: AWD has front, rear, and center differentials (3 total). RWD: single rear diff. FWD: single front diff. High-power RWD: lower acceleration values (toward 25-40%). High-power FWD: lower acceleration values (toward 15-20%). Deceleration off-throttle only; acceleration on-throttle only.

categories.formula: No explicit gearing formulas provided in guide. Article references 'Introduction to Gear Tuning in Forza' as separate topic. Guide focuses on suspension, aero, brakes, differentials only.
categories.concreteNumbers: None provided (separate article required)
categories.goalVariation: Not addressed in this guide
categories.edgeCases: Gearing is mentioned but detailed tuning deferred to 'Introduction to Gear Tuning in Forza'

categories.formula: Roll Center Height Offset: lower roll center height creates more lean; higher reduces lean. Tight tracks typically want higher offset (more responsiveness). Long sweeping curves want lower offset. Start with ARB stiffening up to 20-25 first; if still unresponsive, increase roll center equally front/rear by ~1 inch. Anti-Dive/Anti-Squat: start with dampers first. If excessive dive (braking) or squat (acceleration) remains, increase anti-dive or anti-squat slightly (small changes 2-3% recommended). Excessive anti-dive/squat can make car rigid/unresponsive in corners.
categories.concreteNumbers: Roll center: start ~1 inch adjustment. ARB threshold: 20-25 value before roll center needed. Anti-dive/anti-squat: small increments; default often effective.
categories.goalVariation: Tight tracks: higher roll center offset for responsiveness. Sweeping curves: lower offset for balance. Dive control: increase anti-dive if excessive. Squat control: increase anti-squat if excessive.
categories.edgeCases: FH6/Motorsport 2023+ only feature. Roll center and camber interact — increasing roll center may require adjusting camber. Must verify suspension behavior after geometry changes.

reliability: Moderate. The source provides concrete numeric ranges and baselines (e.g., tire PSI, camber degrees, brake bias percentages, differential percentages, damping values, mechanical balance targets). However, these are empirically tuned recommendations, not derived formulas. Most values depend on live testing feedback (especially mechanical balance readout) rather than mathematical functions of car properties. No explicit car-input dependencies (weight, power, CG) are disclosed; recommendations vary by discipline and drivetrain type only. The guide is practical and detailed but treats tuning as iterative adjustment, not calculation.

categories.formula: Pressure: stock/street/rally 26–28 PSI front and rear; semi-slick/slick/drift 30–33 PSI front and rear. Adjust by 1–2 PSI if sliding (lower) or overheating (raise).
categories.concreteNumbers: 26–28 PSI (street), 30–33 PSI (performance), adjust ±1–2 PSI per symptom. Drift front tire near max, rear near min (asymmetric).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 28 PSI balanced. Drag: max front, min rear. Drift: max front, low rear. Off-road: compound selection primary; pressure secondary.
categories.edgeCases: Compound type (stock/semi-slick/slick/rally/off-road) is the primary factor; no mention of weight or load distribution affecting pressure.

categories.formula: Camber (road racing): –1.5° to –2.5° front, –1.0° to –2.0° rear. Camber (drift): up to –3.0° front, rear near 0°. Toe (front): 0.1° to 0.3° toe-out for turn-in sharpness; rear: small toe-in for stability, near 0° preferred. Caster (road racing): 6.0° to 7.0°; guide recommends 7.0° as default starting point.
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber front –1.5 to –2.5° (circuit), –3.0° (drift). Camber rear –1.0 to –2.0° (circuit), ≈0° (drift). Toe front 0.1–0.3° (toe-out), rear near 0°. Caster 7.0° (road racing); default stock is 5.0°.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: –1.5 to –2.5° front / –1.0 to –2.0° rear. Drag: slight negative front, slight positive rear. Drift: –3.0° front, near 0° rear. Off-road: not specified, treat as circuit baseline.
categories.edgeCases: No variation by drivetrain; no weight or corner-load inputs provided. Toe behavior described as 'unpredictable in Forza physics,' suggesting empirical tuning required.

categories.formula: No numeric spring rate formula given. Qualitative guidance: smooth tarmac = stiffer; mountain passes = moderate; off-road = soft. Match stiffness to weight distribution (heavier end = stiffer springs).
categories.concreteNumbers: No concrete spring rate values (e.g., N/mm) provided. Only relative stiffness guidance (stiff/moderate/soft).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (tarmac): stiff. Touge/mountains: moderate. Off-road: soft. Drag: stiff front, soft rear. Drift: soft overall.
categories.edgeCases: Qualitative match to weight distribution; no formula for absolute stiffness per unit weight.

categories.formula: Start at minimum ride height; raise only if bottoming out repeatedly. No explicit numeric values provided.
categories.concreteNumbers: Minimum setting as baseline; off-road and rally require raising above minimum. No millimeter values specified.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: minimum. Drag: minimum front, slightly above minimum rear. Off-road: raised above minimum for suspension travel. Rally: raised above minimum.
categories.edgeCases: Off-road requires more ground clearance; city/touge may not. No weight or suspension stiffness inputs.

categories.formula: Mechanical Balance target: 0.55 to 0.65, with 0.60 as the sweet spot for road racing. Below 0.55 = understeer; above 0.65 = instability. Tuning method: 'Max out both ARBs, then soften from there until the balance number and driving feel are both right.' Off-road: soften both ARBs toward minimum (opposite of tarmac).
categories.concreteNumbers: Mechanical balance target 0.55–0.65 (circuit), sweet spot 0.60. Off-road: both ARBs minimum or near-minimum. No explicit stiffness values (e.g., N/mm) provided; tuning is feedback-based.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: hit 0.60 balance by adjusting stiffness. Drift: soft front, stiff rear. Off-road: both minimum. Drag: soft front, stiff rear.
categories.edgeCases: Mechanical Balance stat is game-specific feedback loop; no real-world ARB stiffness formula. Off-road tuning inverts tarmac logic.

categories.formula: Universal baseline: rebound 18.0, bump 6.0 (front and rear). Ratio rule: rebound should be significantly higher than bump. Adjustments by symptom: unresponsive front entry = lower front damping slightly; unstable rear = lower rear damping slightly; bouncing = increase rebound slightly; too reactive = lower rebound.
categories.concreteNumbers: Baseline: rebound 18.0 / bump 6.0 (both front and rear). Rebound ≫ bump (ratio ~3:1 typical). Adjustments in small increments per feel.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 18 rebound / 6 bump as baseline. Drift: soften slightly from baseline for stability. Drag: stiffer (exact values not given). Off-road: softer than tarmac for large suspension movements.
categories.edgeCases: No explicit formula; all adjustments are iterative feedback-based. No inputs for weight, spring rate, or suspension travel.

categories.formula: Balance target: 0.40–0.45 window for road racing; start rear aero until 'around 0.50 on the balance readout.' Japan Touge/City: moderate downforce works best.
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero balance 0.40–0.45 (circuit sweet spot). Start by adding rear aero until balance reads ~0.50, then adjust downforce level. No specific downforce (kg or lbs) values provided.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 0.40–0.45 balance. Touge/city: moderate (no specific target). Drag: minimal or removed. Off-road: minimal. Drift: minimal.
categories.edgeCases: No speed or weight dependency; tuning is balance-stat driven. Game-specific feedback loop.

categories.formula: Balance: 50–55% front bias for most road racing; drift builds use more front bias to prevent rear lockup mid-drift. Pressure: start at 100%; lower if locking, raise if slow to decelerate.
categories.concreteNumbers: Brake bias 50–55% front (circuit), front-biased for drift (exact % not specified, but more aggressive). Pressure 100% baseline, adjust down if locking, up if insufficient decel.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 50–55% front. Drift: front-biased (70%+implied). Off-road: not specified. Drag: not specified.
categories.edgeCases: No formula; depends on driving style (lock tendency) and surface type. Drift requires heavier front bias to prevent rear lockup during angle hold.

categories.formula: RWD acceleration: 50–60% for road racing, 70–90% for drift. RWD deceleration: 10–20% for road racing. FWD acceleration: 60–80%. FWD deceleration: 10–20%. AWD center balance (road racing): 60–90% rear, most setups 70–80% rear. AWD center balance (rally/off-road): 50–70% rear. Hard rule: never drop below 50% rear.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD circuit: 50–60% accel, 10–20% decel. RWD drift: 70–90% accel, decel not specified. FWD: 60–80% accel, 10–20% decel. AWD circuit: 70–80% rear, road racing 60–90% rear, rally 50–70% rear. Absolute floor: 50% rear.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (RWD): 55% accel / 15% decel typical. Circuit (AWD): 70–80% rear. Drift (RWD): 70–90% accel. Rally/off-road (AWD): 50–70% rear. Drag (RWD): ~85% accel (inferred).
categories.edgeCases: Values vary strictly by drivetrain (RWD/FWD/AWD) and discipline. No weight, power, or torque curve inputs provided.

categories.formula: Final drive logic: shorter (toward acceleration) = faster response on tight tracks; longer (toward speed) = higher top speed. Tuning goal: 'Set final drive so the car hits redline near the fastest point of the circuit.' Drift gearing: 'Pick your main drift gear, throttle from a dead stop, adjust final drive until you hit redline smoothly.'
categories.concreteNumbers: No explicit final-drive ratio values (e.g., 3.2:1 vs 4.1:1); only relative guidance (shorter/longer). Drift: hit redline at optimal drift speed for chosen gear.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (tight): bias toward shorter/acceleration ratio. Circuit (high-speed): longer/top-speed ratio. Drag: likely max short ratio (not specified). Drift: match to drift gear redline speed.
categories.edgeCases: No formula for optimal ratio per track or power curve. Tuning is iterative based on redline point observation and desired response.

reliability: Mixed — contains practical heuristics and starting-point values (e.g. PSI ranges, camber degrees) but lacks precision on exact formulas. Most recommendations are qualitative rules ("if car doesn't turn, reduce front camber") rather than hard equations. Treat as a guide with empirically-tested starting values, not a mathematical formula database.

categories.formula: Start AWD at 28 PSI front / 27-28 PSI rear; adjust by 0.5 PSI increments. Trucks/Heavy baseline: 32 PSI.
categories.goalVariation: High pressure = quick/responsive but slides; low pressure = grips but sluggish. Circuit racing typically uses mid-range; drag may favor higher pressures for responsiveness.
inputs: vehicle type (race car, street car, truck, off-roader)
categories.concreteNumbers: 28 PSI front, 27-28 PSI rear (AWD); 32 PSI (trucks/heavy); 0.5 PSI increment adjustments

categories.formula: Softer suspension aids grip; lower ride height improves cornering. Race cars: 4-6; Street cars: 5-7; Prototypes: 3.5-4.5; Off-roaders: 5-7.
categories.goalVariation: Most AWD builds use softest or near-softest spring settings. Start at lower range; raise only if serious handling issues.
categories.concreteNumbers: Ride height ranges by type: Race 4-6, Street 5-7, Prototype 3.5-4.5, Off-road 5-7

categories.formula: Race cars: 4-6; Street cars: 5-7; Prototypes: 3.5-4.5; Off-roaders: 5-7. Logic: Start at lower range; raise only if serious handling issues.
categories.goalVariation: Lower height improves cornering; higher provides better ride compliance for rough terrain.
categories.concreteNumbers: Race 4-6, Street 5-7, Prototype 3.5-4.5, Off-road 5-7

categories.formula: Negative camber essential for cornering grip. AWD baseline: Front camber -1.5 to -2.0°, Rear camber -1.0 to -1.5°. Toe: keep near zero. Caster: keep above 5.5° for racing.
categories.goalVariation: Higher caster (above 5.5°) improves stability + grip for circuit racing. Toe adjustments only if car feels unstable (rare).
categories.concreteNumbers: Front camber -1.5 to -2.0°, Rear camber -1.0 to -1.5° (AWD); Caster >5.5° for racing; Toe near 0°

categories.formula: Stiffer generally better for AWD racing. Lower front ARB = front gets more grip (reduces understeer). Lower rear ARB = rear grippier and stable (reduces oversteer). Max out both for most AWD builds; lower selectively if understeer/oversteer persists.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing typically maximizes both. Off-road and loose-surface builds may require lower values for compliance.
categories.concreteNumbers: No absolute values given; qualitative: 'max out both for most AWD builds'; adjust selectively downward

categories.formula: Baseline: Front/Rear at 18.0 rebound / 6.0 bump. Rebound controls suspension return speed (keep much higher than bump). Bump controls compression speed. Adjustments: bouncy car → increase rebound slightly; too sensitive → lower rebound. Corner entry issues → lower front damping. Rear instability → lower rear damping.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing uses baseline or slight increases to rebound. Off-road/rough terrain may require lower damping for compliance.
categories.concreteNumbers: 18.0 rebound / 6.0 bump baseline

categories.formula: Balance front vs. rear to prevent spin-out. Start at 10% front increase. More front brake = safer/stable; more rear = easier cornering but spin-risk. Brake force: keep mid-range; high = stronger but lock-prone; low = easier control.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing starts at 10% front bias (more front). Drift may favor higher rear bias. Drag needs careful balance to avoid wheel-lock.
categories.concreteNumbers: 10% front increase baseline

categories.formula: Acceleration: keep as high as possible; lower only if excessive understeer on exit. Deceleration: lower baseline; raise if corner entry instability. Center Balance (AWD only): more front = safer/easier but understeer; more rear = faster/aggressive but oversteer-risk. RWD: adjust rear differential only. FWD: adjust front differential only.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing: maximize acceleration, balance center bias to neutralize. Drift: favor rear bias for oversteer. Drag: high acceleration for launch grip.
categories.concreteNumbers: No absolute percentages; qualitative guidance (maximize acceleration, lower deceleration baseline, balance center bias by goal)

categories.formula: More downforce = grip; less = speed. Front: maximize downforce for cornering grip (sacrifices straight-line speed). Rear: minimize downforce for maximum speed; increase only if rear slides. Trade-off: more aero = grip; less aero = speed.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing: maximize front downforce, minimize rear (or balance for stability). Drag: minimize total aero for speed. Drift: moderate aero for controllability.
categories.concreteNumbers: No absolute values; qualitative guidance based on grip vs speed trade-off

categories.formula: Balance final drive for top speed vs. acceleration. Primary goal: 'Make your car barely hit top speed at end of longest straight.' Adjustment logic: Bouncing off limiter early → increase speed; Slow corner exit → increase acceleration.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing: optimize for track layout (frequent short straights vs few long straights). Drag: maximize top speed. Touge/Drift: favor acceleration.
categories.concreteNumbers: No exact formula; rule-based: 'barely hit top speed at end of longest straight'

reliability: Low — guide explicitly avoids concrete numeric specifications, states "Forget about absolute numbers" for spring stiffness. Only 5 sparse numeric values found across entire guide; rest is qualitative guidance (soften/stiffen). Not suitable as reference for tuning calculator.

categories.concreteNumbers: 28-30 PSI (front only)
categories.goalVariation: Not specified by goal; single context given (understeer correction)

reliability: Concrete formulas and numeric ranges present for alignment, springs/damping, ARB, braking, differential, and aero. No concrete gearing, turbo, or advanced engine tuning formulas found in accessible sources. Nexus Mods mod 113 (F.A.T.T.Y) page inaccessible (HTTP 403). Primary forza.guide blocked. Extracted data is genuine but incomplete coverage of all tuning categories.

categories.formula: Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Slider Position %) + Min Rate. Weight Distribution Adjustment: Shift roughly 4 kgf/mm of spring rate per 1% deviation from 50/50 weight distribution.
categories.concreteNumbers: Sports class RWD/AWD: Front 87-98%, Rear 58-80%; High Performance: Front 85-93%, Rear 63-84%; Race Car: Front 83-93%, Rear 59-85%. FWD front/rear values reversed.
categories.goalVariation: Rally: Halve the Race Spring values. Off-Road: Slider positions ~39-40% front and 6-7% rear.
categories.edgeCases: FWD cars swap front/rear slider targets compared to RWD/AWD. Off-road and rally disciplines require significantly different settings.

categories.formula: Front Bump = Min Bump + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6. Rear Damping Offset based on spring rate difference: 0-1.5% (Rear Rebound -0.2, Rear Bump -0.1); 1.5-35% (Rear Rebound -0.3, Rear Bump -0.2); 36-40% (Rear Rebound -0.6, Rear Bump -0.4); >40% (Rear Rebound -1.2, Rear Bump -0.8).
categories.concreteNumbers: Min Bump by class: Sports 4.4-4.8, High Performance 4.5-4.9, Race Car 4.7-4.9. Default recommended: 18.0/6.0 (Rebound/Bump) for both front and rear. Rally/Off-Road: Add +1.0 to rebound across the board.
categories.goalVariation: Rally and off-road increase rebound damping by +1.0 across all positions.
categories.edgeCases: Heavier vehicles use higher minimum bump values. Spring rate difference significantly affects rear damping offsets.

categories.formula: No formulas provided. Specific angle recommendations by discipline:
categories.concreteNumbers: Road Racing (Asphalt): Front -1.0 to -2.0°, Rear -0.5 to -1.0°. Dirt Rally: Front -0.8 to -1.2°, Rear -0.5 to -0.8°. Cross Country: Front -0.5°, Rear -0.5°. Drift: Front -3.0 to -5.0°, Rear -1.0°. Toe: Default 0.0° both ends; Front toe-out -0.1 to -0.2° or Rear toe-in +0.1 to +0.3° only as last resort. Caster: 5.0-7.0° (Light 5.0-5.5°, Mid 5.5-6.5°, Heavy 6.5-7.0°).
categories.goalVariation: Drift requires front camber of -3.0 to -5.0° (significantly more negative). Off-road allows more neutral settings. Road racing uses moderate negative camber.
categories.edgeCases: Drift requires extreme negative camber on front. Cross-country uses neutral alignment. Toe should remain at 0.0° except when correcting understeer/oversteer.

categories.formula: Base ARB = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Front/Rear Distribution: RWD +1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%, FWD -1 per 1%, AWD +0.66 per 1%.
categories.concreteNumbers: Class stiffness percentages: Sports 61-65%, High Performance 40-46%, Race Car 35-62%. Discipline targets: RWD Front 18-25 / Rear 25-35, AWD Front 22-30 / Rear 28-38, FWD Front 8-15 / Rear 25-40.
categories.goalVariation: Off-road and rally typically use lower overall ARB stiffness. FWD cars use much lower front ARB (8-15) compared to RWD (18-25).
categories.edgeCases: FWD requires opposite distribution logic (-1 per 1% weight above 50%). Heavier vehicles may require higher base values.

categories.formula: No formulas provided. Discipline-specific slider position targets:
categories.concreteNumbers: Road Racing: Minimum both ends. Dirt Rally: 70-80% of slider range. Cross Country: Maximum both ends. Drag: Minimum front, slightly higher rear. General starting points: Race Cars 4-6, Street Cars 5-7, Prototypes 3.5-4.5, Off-roaders 5-7.
categories.goalVariation: Drag racing uses asymmetric height (low front). Road racing uses minimum. Off-road uses maximum. Rally uses 70-80% range.
categories.edgeCases: Drag racing requires front-biased low height for launch traction.

categories.formula: No formulas provided. Absolute pressure values:
categories.concreteNumbers: AWD baseline: 28 PSI front, 27-28 PSI rear. Heavy vehicles/trucks: 32 PSI. Racing cars: ~27 or 32 PSI (99% of cases). Adjustment increments: Half PSI at a time.
categories.goalVariation: Racing uses 27-32 PSI depending on car. Heavy vehicles use 32 PSI. Light racing cars use ~27 PSI.
categories.edgeCases: Heavy vehicles require higher pressures. Racing cars typically use either 27 or 32 PSI extremes.

categories.formula: No formulas provided. Specific brake balance percentages by drivetrain and discipline:
categories.concreteNumbers: Road Racing: RWD 50-55% front bias, AWD 52-56% front bias, FWD 55-62% front bias. Drift/Dirt: 45-50% (rear-biased). Brake Pressure: 100% default, Range 70-200%.
categories.goalVariation: Drift and dirt use rear bias (45-50%). FWD uses highest front bias (55-62%). Road racing RWD uses lowest front bias (50-55%).
categories.edgeCases: Drift requires rear bias for controlled oversteer. FWD requires highest front bias for stopping power. AWD sits between RWD and FWD.

categories.formula: No formulas provided. Specific lock percentages by drivetrain and discipline:
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD Road Racing: Acceleration 40-65%, Deceleration 15-30%. RWD Drift: Acceleration 80-100%. AWD Road Racing: Front Accel 28% / Decel 0%, Rear Accel 100% / Decel 45%, Center 70-85% rear bias. AWD Dirt Rally: Front Accel 30-40% / Decel 0-15%, Rear Accel 55-70% / Decel 20-25%, Center 65-75% rear bias. AWD Cross Country: Front Accel 35-45% / Decel 5-15%, Rear Accel 65-75% / Decel 20-25%, Center 55-65% rear bias.
categories.goalVariation: Drift uses maximum lock (80-100% acceleration). Road racing uses moderate locks. Dirt rally uses front-biased settings. Cross country uses mild rear bias. Wheelbase affects tolerance: longer cars up to 90% rear bias, shorter cars 70-80%.
categories.edgeCases: AWD requires complex center balance; long wheelbase tolerates 90% rear bias vs short wheelbase 70-80%. Drift requires near-full lock. Coast lock (0-15% range typical) vs acceleration (40-100% range).

categories.formula: No formulas provided. Aero balance targets and downforce slider ranges by drivetrain and discipline:
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance (front:total ratio) — Road Racing AWD: 0.40-0.45, RWD: 0.50-0.55, FWD: 0.45-0.55. Drift: 0.45-0.50. Dirt/Cross Country: ~0.50. Front Downforce: AWD Maximum, RWD 60-80% slider, FWD High-to-Maximum. Rear Downforce: AWD Minimum, RWD 60-90%, FWD Low-to-Moderate. Aero Efficiency: Excellent >0.85, Average 0.70-0.85, Poor <0.70.
categories.goalVariation: AWD uses front-biased aero (0.40-0.45) to fight understeer. RWD uses neutral (0.50-0.55). FWD slightly front-biased (0.45-0.55). Drift slightly front-biased (0.45-0.50).
categories.edgeCases: Race aero adds 15-30 kg. Off-road downforce rarely exceeds 150 km/h effectiveness threshold. AWD uses minimum rear + maximum front. FWD uses high-to-maximum front downforce.

categories.formula: No concrete formulas available in accessible sources. Community consensus: adjust final drive first (moves all gears uniformly), then fine-tune individual gears. Middle gears should follow logarithmic spacing curve (larger 1st-2nd gap, tighter at top).
categories.concreteNumbers: Higher final drive = shorter gears, faster acceleration, lower top speed. Lower final drive = taller gears, higher top speed, slower acceleration. No numeric baselines provided.
categories.goalVariation: Drag racing uses very short final drive for maximum acceleration. Circuit racing balances acceleration and top speed. No discipline-specific numeric targets found.
categories.edgeCases: No formulas available for calculating gear ratios based on weight, power, or engine displacement.

reliability: High for formulas extracted from official Forza forums and community-vetted guides (ForzaFire, ForzaTune, QuickTune). Spring and damping formulas are physics-based and consistent across Forza titles. Gearing and differential ranges are empirical starting points verified across multiple sources. Tire pressures are baseline PSI values with weight adjustments confirmed in multiple guides.

categories.formula: Spring Rate = [(2π)² ÷ t²] × M, where M (mass) = [(Weight ÷ Front Weight %) × 0.5] ÷ 32 (front) or [Weight × (1 - Front Weight %)] ÷ 64 (rear), t = time constant (1.25s, 1.50s, or 1.75s)
categories.concreteNumbers: K multiplier values: t=1.25: K=0.394784; t=1.50: K=0.274156; t=1.75: K=0.201420. Alternative: Front Spring = K × Weight × FrontWeightDistribution; Rear Spring = K × Weight - FrontSpring. Slider position targets (RWD examples): Sports 87-98% front / 58-80% rear; High Performance 85-93% front / 63-84% rear; Race 83-93% front / 59-85% rear. Adjustment: shift ~4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50 weight distribution.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (Road race): minimum ride height, use K=0.394784 for responsive feel. Drag: adjust for weight bias (heavier rear may need softer rear springs). Drift: stiffer springs reduce body roll but lower grip window. Rally: 1.50-1.75s (softer) for bump absorption. Off-Road: maximum spring rates to handle terrain.
categories.edgeCases: RWD vs FWD: FWD reverses front/rear slider targets (softer front). AWD: intermediate between RWD/FWD. Heavier vehicles (>1500 kg) require higher absolute spring rates; lighter vehicles (<1000 kg) need lower rates. Chassis reinforcement reduces required springs: Sport -2.75%, Race -5.5%.

categories.formula: For standard Forza 6-speed race gearbox: (400 - Car HP) ÷ 600 + 4.25 = Final Drive. For custom gearbox: (Stock HP - Current HP) ÷ 600 + Stock FD = Final Drive. Alternative method: (Weight ÷ HP) × (1st gear - Last gear) ÷ 100 + Stock FD
categories.concreteNumbers: Example 1: 325 hp car: (400-325)÷600+4.25 = 4.38 FD. Example 2: 325 hp (stock 300), stock FD 3.30: (300-325)÷600+3.30 = 3.26 FD. Standard reference gearbox ratios: 2.89, 1.99, 1.49, 1.19, 0.94, 0.78
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: tune FD so car reaches rev limiter in top gear at end of longest straight. Drag: lower FD (shorter gears) for acceleration. Rally: higher FD (taller gears) for top speed on straights. Drift: depends on course layout—typically mid-range. Road race vs off-road minimal FD difference.
categories.edgeCases: Power scaling: more power = lower FD (shorter gears); less power = higher FD (taller gears). Custom gearbox vs stock: custom allows fine-tuning per-gear; final drive still follows horsepower relationship. AWD vs RWD: same formula applies; weight effect minimal.

categories.formula: Base ARB = (Weight in lb ÷ 2) ÷ (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Distribution: +1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50% (RWD); -1 ARB per 1% (FWD); +0.66 per 1% (AWD).
categories.concreteNumbers: Example: 2500 lb car, 63% stiffness, 1.00 distribution: (2500÷2)÷(200-126) = 16.89 ARB. Class stiffness targets: Sports 61-65%, High Performance 40-46%, Race 35-62%. Rule of thumb: ±0.5 ARB per 1% weight distribution variance from 50%.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road race): 40-65% stiffness for progressive understeer correction. Drift: 35-45% (soft) for controlled slides. Rally: 50-60% (moderate) for terrain variance. Drag: 30-40% (very soft) minimal roll effect needed. Off-Road: 35-45% soft to allow suspension travel.
categories.edgeCases: RWD front-heavy: increase front ARB more; RWD rear-heavy: increase rear ARB more. FWD typically wants softer front ARB (weight distribution reversed). AWD 0.66 multiplier reflects better traction distribution. Heavier vehicles (>1500 kg) use higher absolute ARB values; lighter (<1000 kg) use lower values.

categories.formula: Rebound/Bump = ((WB% (lbs) × 9.8) ÷ (Spring Rate × Ride Height²)) + 1. Front-specific: Front Bump = Min Bump (class) + (Front Weight in lb ÷ 200) × 0.1; Front Rebound = Front Bump ÷ 0.6. Rear offset table based on spring rate differential.
categories.concreteNumbers: Min Bump by class: Sports 4.4-4.8, High Performance 4.5-4.9, Race 4.7-4.9. Example: 3000 lb car, 52% front, 4.5 min bump: 4.5 + (1560÷200)×0.1 = 5.28 front bump; Rebound = 5.28÷0.6 = 8.8. Rear offsets: 0-1.5% SR diff: -0.2 rebound/-0.1 bump; 1.5-35%: -0.3/-0.2; 36-40%: -0.6/-0.4; >40%: -1.2/-0.8. Baseline: Bump = 40-70% of Rebound, target ~60%.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 55-65% bump ratio for balanced absorption/extension. Drag: 40-50% (softer bump) for weight transfer. Drift: 50-60% for controlled response. Rally: 55-70% to handle variable terrain. Off-Road: 45-60% for compliance.
categories.edgeCases: RWD heavier front: increase front bump; lighter rear: decrease rear offsets. FWD inverts weight logic (softer front damping). AWD balanced approach. High spring rate differential (>35%): apply larger rear offsets. Per 100 lb front weight change: adjust rebound ±0.1, bump inversely.

categories.formula: No direct mathematical formula. Empirical tuning ranges based on drivetrain and goal. Acceleration lock % = torque distribution when accelerating; Deceleration lock % = torque coupling when braking.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: Acceleration 40-65% (road racing), Deceleration 30-40%. High-performance/race RWD: Accel 70%, Decel 34%. Prototype: Accel 96%, Decel 0%. GP race: Accel 0%, Decel 80%. Drift: Accel 65-90% (65% min for consistent lines, below 70% twitchy). FWD: Accel 50-70%, Decel 20-30%. AWD: Center Balance typically 40-60% rear torque split.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road): 40-65% accel for progressive traction. Drag: higher accel% (70-80%) for initial launch traction. Drift: 80-90% for welded feel. Rally: 50-70% varies per stage. Off-Road: 50-60% variable terrain.
categories.edgeCases: RWD vs FWD: RWD can go higher accel%; FWD needs lower (risk of understeer). AWD center balance affects front/rear 50/50 coupling. Turbo/supercharged high-power cars benefit from lower accel% to prevent wheelspin.

categories.formula: No direct mathematical formula. Empirical angle targets by discipline. Camber adjustment primarily affects mid-corner grip; Toe affects turn-in response; Caster affects straight-line stability and steering feel.
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber: Road race Front -1.0° to -2.0° (use -2.0° for slicks, -1.0° for sport), Rear -0.5° to -1.0°. Dirt rally Front -0.8° to -1.2°, Rear -0.5° to -0.8°. Cross country Front/Rear -0.5°. Drift Front -3.0° to -5.0°, Rear -1.0°. Toe: keep 0.0° (neutral), adjust only as last resort (-0.1° to +0.3°). Caster: 5.0° to 7.0° range; 5.0° baseline, 6.0°-7.0° for modern cars and road race.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: negative camber (-1.5° avg) for cornering grip. Drag: minimal camber (-0.5°), prioritize straight-line stability. Drift: aggressive front camber (-4.0°) to maintain grip at high steering angles. Rally: moderate (-1.0°) for variable surfaces. Off-Road: minimal (-0.5°) flatter contact patch.
categories.edgeCases: High-power RWD: increase negative camber to manage weight transfer. FWD: front camber critical (driven wheel); may need -1.5° minimum. Slick tires: tolerate and benefit from more negative camber (-2.0°+). Street/sport tires: less aggressive (-1.0°). Static camber should equal ~0° dynamic camber at corner apex during acceleration for max grip.

categories.formula: No mathematical formula. Slider position targets by discipline. Lower = lower center of gravity (less weight transfer, more grip) but reduces suspension travel; Higher = more suspension compliance but higher CG.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road race: minimum both ends. Dirt rally: 70-80% slider range. Cross country: maximum both ends. Drag: minimum front, slightly higher rear. Drift: minimum both ends. Autocross: 60-75% both ends. Note: Forza scales slider ranges by car weight automatically; position percentage already accounts for weight.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: minimum everywhere (lowest CG). Drag: low front (aero balance), higher rear (anti-squat). Drift: minimum (responsiveness). Rally: 70-80% (bump absorption + control). Off-Road: high (suspension travel for terrain).
categories.edgeCases: Very heavy cars (>2000 lb): raising ride height prevents bottoming. Very light cars (<800 kg): can go lower. Low ride height risks suspension lockout if bumps compress springs fully.

categories.formula: Tire pressure baselines: Stock/Street 31.0 PSI, Sport 31.5 PSI, Semi-Slick 32.0 PSI, Race Slicks 32.5 PSI, Rally 29-30 PSI, Off-Road 28-30 PSI, Drift 20-26 PSI. Weight adjustment: heavier vehicles require +5-7 PSI vs light vehicles on same compound.
categories.concreteNumbers: Reference: 600 kg car on sport tires = 28-30 PSI; 1800 kg car on sport tires = 33-35 PSI. Width increase (235→285 rear): +0.5 to +1.0 PSI. Front/Rear split by drivetrain: RWD front +0.5-1.0 PSI higher; FWD front +1.0-2.0 PSI higher; AWD equal or front +0.2-0.5 PSI.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 32-33 PSI (maximize grip). Drag: 32-35 PSI (hotter compound, higher pressure). Drift: 20-26 PSI (smaller contact patch = easier initiation). Rally: 29-30 PSI (compliance on uneven surface). Off-Road: 28-30 PSI (traction on loose surface).
categories.edgeCases: High-downforce aero cars: pressure may go 34-36 PSI (stiff sidewalls). Turbo/supercharged high-heat cars: monitor warm pressures (may exceed baseline). Lower pressure = larger contact patch, more grip but faster wear and heat. Higher pressure = more responsive, less peak grip.

categories.formula: For standard Forza aero kit (RWD): Front Downforce = [Max Front - ((2700 - Weight) ÷ 100)], Rear = [Max Rear - ((2700 - Weight) ÷ 100)]. For custom kits: apply same formula to stock maximum. High power (≥1.5× stock): multiply resulting downforce by 1.5.
categories.concreteNumbers: Example RWD standard kit, 2500 lb: Front = 100 - ((2700-2500)÷100) = 100 - 2 = 98; Rear = 200 - 2 = 198. Reference: 2700 lb baseline. High power (≥1.5× stock): 600 hp = 1.5× multiplier applied to downforce totals.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road race): maximum downforce for cornering grip. Drag: minimal aero (high drag penalty). Drift: low-moderate aero (200-400 total). Rally: minimal (drag on straights hurts acceleration). High-speed circuit (250+ km/h): high downforce (200-400+ rear for stability).
categories.edgeCases: FWD: more sensitive to front aero (affects nose-down balance). AWD: balanced distribution. RWD rear-heavy: may benefit from higher rear wing. FWD front-heavy: may prefer lower front wing. Custom aero kits: same formula applies but on different max values.

categories.formula: No direct mathematical formula for deceleration/balance. Empirical tuning: Brake Balance is front % of total braking force. Distribution affects weight transfer during braking; higher % front = more nose-down, earlier turn-in ability.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: 50-55% front brake bias (balanced). AWD: 52-56% front. FWD: 55-62% front (heavier front = needs more braking bias). Drift/Dirt: 45-50% (rear-biased for weight management). Brake pressure: no tunable formula; ABS on/off selection available.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road race): 50-55% front (balanced mid-corner braking). Drag: minimal braking (low bias %). Drift: 45-50% rear bias (manage weight transfer, drifting requirements). Rally: 50-60% (variable terrain). Trail-braking: might prefer 48-52% (earlier turn-in).
categories.edgeCases: Very heavy RWD (>1500 kg rear): may need 48-52% front (weight distribution). Very light FWD: might use 60%+ front (extreme weight forward). Turbo/high power: higher front bias helps manage braking distance. ABS on: can be more aggressive; ABS off: require careful tuning to avoid lockup.

source: https://forzaquicktune.com/tuning-guide/fm7/part2/ (primary formula source); https://www.forzafire.com/guides/forza-horizon-6-platform-and-handling-tuning-guide (springs, alignment, damping); https://grindout.com/forza-6/guides/tuning (differential, spring, damping, final drive); https://www.forzafire.com/guides/forza-horizon-6-aero-and-appearance-tuning-guide (aero); https://www.forzafire.com/guides/forza-horizon-6-tires-and-rims-tuning-guide (tire pressure)
reliability: High for differential/damping/springs; moderate for gearing/aero (guidelines rather than strict formulas); formulas extracted verbatim from sources, not invented. Note: Some sources (ForzaTune, forza.guide) blocked or empty; used alternative sources with documented formulas.

categories.formula: Accel/Decel are direct percentages (0-100%) with 2% increments. Lock % = wheels forced to spin at similar speeds under throttle (accel) or off-throttle (decel). Higher accel % = more traction on power but more understeer (RWD/FWD) or harder turn-in. Higher decel % = more rear-wheel coupling on lift-off = stable entry, less rotation.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD road race: 40-65% accel / 15-30% decel. RWD drift: 80-100% accel / 10-40% decel. FWD road race: 80-95% accel / 5-10% decel. AWD road race: Front 80-95% accel / 5-10% decel, Rear 50-70% accel / 10-15% decel, Center 70-80% rear bias. AWD Touge: Front 15-25% accel / 5-10% decel, Rear 65-80% accel / 15-20% decel, Center 70-80% rear. Accel increments: 2% only (always use even numbers).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: RWD 50-60% accel / 10-20% decel; AWD rear 55-75%, center 70-80%. Drag: Accel ~85%, decel irrelevant. Drift: RWD 95-100% accel / 10-40% decel (baseline 100% accel / 10% decel). Rally: Moderate accel (50-70%), moderate decel (15-25%). Touge (mountain): RWD higher accel (70-80%), AWD front/rear split varies sharply.
categories.edgeCases: AWD: never center diff below 50%. FWD: accel max 95% (restricts turn-in), decel min 5% (instability). RWD: pure drift uses 100% accel. Drag: decel lock irrelevant. All drivetrains: decel primarily affects off-throttle entry stability, not exit.

categories.formula: Final Drive (Custom Gearbox) = Stock FD + ((Stock Power - Current Power) / 6) × 0.01. Alternative formula (Standard): FD = 4.25 + ((400 - Current Power in HP) / 6) × 0.01. Each individual gear ratio adjusts proportionally by final drive multiplier.
categories.concreteNumbers: 250 HP ≈ FD 4.50. 400 HP = FD 4.25 (reference). 600 HP ≈ FD 3.91. Per-discipline offsets: Rally +0.50, Off-road +0.75, Drag -0.20, Drift +0.10 to base calculations. Goal is engine power band usage: choose gears to keep engine spinning in power band for best acceleration across speed range.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Standard formula applies. Drag: FD -0.20 offset (taller gears, lower accel). Drift: FD +0.10 offset (shorter gears). Rally: FD +0.50 offset. Off-road: FD +0.75 offset. Touge: similar to circuit but slightly taller (slower accel/higher top speed for technical sections).
categories.edgeCases: Custom gearbox requires stock FD input; standard forza boxes use 4.25 reference. AWD power distribution affects effective power input. Engine position (mid-engine, front, rear) affects RPM at given speed (tire diameter compensation). Tire width changes effective diameter slightly, requires recalculation.
