categories.formula: Spring Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Slider Position %) + Min Rate. Alternative: Rate = (Total Weight / 2) × (Weight Distribution %) × (Spring % Range). Per 10lb front downforce: +0.5 spring rate. Per 25lb rear downforce: +0.5 spring rate. Adjustment for weight shift: ~4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50 weight distribution.
categories.concreteNumbers: Sports class: Front 87-98%, Rear 58-80% slider (RWD/AWD); High Performance: Front 85-93%, Rear 63-84%; Race Car: Front 83-93%, Rear 59-85%. Example calculation: 3000lb car, 52% front, 86% slider = 670; 100% slider = 780. Rally springs ~50% of Race values. Off-road: ~39-40% front, ~6-7% rear.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: High front (87-98%), moderate rear (58-85%). Drag: moderate front/rear (around 70% each), slight rake (lower rear). Drift: Front 50%, Rear 85% (enables rotation). Rally: ~50% of race values. Off-road: ~40% front / 6% rear (compliance). Road race minimum both ends.
categories.edgeCases: RWD vs AWD: sliders reversed for FWD (front lower, rear higher). Rally springs are intrinsically softer (~50% of race springs). Off-road must be extremely soft. Aero adds downforce: increasing spring rate slightly per lb added. Weight change requires proportional spring adjustment.

categories.formula: Bump = 40-70% of Rebound, with ~60% as baseline. Front Bump = Min Bump (class-based) + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6 (inverts the 60% ratio). Rear Rebound offset from front based on spring rate difference: 0-1.5% diff = -0.2 rear rebound / -0.1 rear bump; 1.5-35% diff = -0.3 rear rebound / -0.2 rear bump.
categories.concreteNumbers: Min Bump by class: Sports 4.4-4.8, High Perf 4.5-4.9, Race Car 4.7-4.9. Example 1400kg car (52% front) on Sports: Bump = 4.6 + 0.93 = 5.5, Rebound = 5.5/0.6 = 9.2. Per 100lb weight reduction: rebound +0.1, bump -0.1. Circuit baseline: ~8 bump / ~14 rebound. Drift: ~4/4/4/4 all soft. Street: ~4 bump / ~6 rebound.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Bump ~8, Rebound ~14 (60% ratio). Drag: Stiff (bump ~10, rebound ~16). Drift: Very soft (~4/4). Street: Soft (~4/6). Rally: Moderate (~5-6 bump / 8-10 rebound). Off-road: Very soft (~1/3). Ratio always 40-70% bumps/rebound; 60% is sweet spot.
categories.edgeCases: Heavy cars need higher base bump. Light cars (< 2500 lb) use lower minimums. Weight transfer under load = higher bump needed. Spring rate differences between front/rear create rear offset. Mid-engine cars shift weight distribution, requiring custom bump calculations.

categories.formula: Camber angle = front/rear tilt (negative = inward lean at top). Toe = front/rear angle left-right (negative = toe-out front, positive = toe-in rear). Caster = kingpin angle (5.0-7.0° typical). No explicit mathematical formula; values are direct angle inputs (degrees). Caster adjustment: lighter cars use 5-6°, standard 6.5-7.0°.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road racing asphalt: Camber Front -1.0° to -2.0°, Rear -0.5° to -1.0° (use -2.0° for slicks, -1.0° for sport). Dirt rally: Front -0.8° to -1.2°, Rear -0.5° to -0.8°. Cross country: Front -0.5°, Rear -0.5°. Drift: Front -3.0° to -5.0°, Rear -1.0°. Caster: 5.0-7.0° depending on weight. Toe: 0.0° default racing; front toe-out -0.1° to -0.2° for turn-in; rear toe-in +0.1° to +0.3°. Open-wheel: rear camber ~1.0° less negative than standard.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Front -1.0° to -2.0°, Rear -0.5° to -1.0° (high-grip). Drag: minimal camber (near 0°). Drift: Aggressive (-3.0° to -5.0° front). Rally: Moderate (-0.8° to -1.2° front). Off-road: Minimal (-0.5°). Street: -1.5° front / -1.0° rear.
categories.edgeCases: Wider tires = reduce camber (flatter contact patch). Narrower tires = increase negative camber (compensate for deflection). Asphalt needs more camber than dirt. Grip mode racing: toe at 0.0° (unpredictable in standard); drift-only uses toe adjustments. More negative camber = more cornering grip but less straight-line braking/acceleration grip.

categories.formula: Base ARB = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × ARB Stiffness %). Per weight distribution offset: RWD = +1.0 per 1% front weight deviation; FWD = -1.0 per 1%; AWD = +0.66 per 1%. Final Front/Rear = Base ± (Offset / 2).
categories.concreteNumbers: Example 2500lb car, 63% stiffness: Base = (2500/2) / (200 - 200×0.63) = 16.89. RWD: Front 18-25, Rear 25-35. AWD: Front 22-30, Rear 28-38. FWD: Front 8-15, Rear 25-40. Per weight distribution: RWD +1 per 1% front (heavier front = stiffer front ARB needed). FWD -1 per 1%. AWD +0.66 per 1%.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Standard ranges above. Drag: Very stiff (max slider). Drift: Softer front to allow lean (lower front, stiffer rear). Rally: Moderate. Off-road: Soft (lower values for compliance). Touge: Tuned to minimize understeer (higher front for RWD/AWD).
categories.edgeCases: The 1/65 ARB exploit (minimum front / maximum rear) used on AWD to overcome understeer. Weight distribution heavily influences distribution ratio. FWD front ARBs are naturally soft due to formula. AWD benefit from stiffer fronts proportionally.

categories.formula: Ride height has no explicit formula; it is slider position applied as a percentage of range. Target depends on goal: minimum = 0%, maximum = 100%. Adjustments affect center of gravity (lower = lower CoG), ground clearance, and aero angle.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road race: Minimum both ends (0-10%). Dirt rally: 70-80% of range (ground clearance). Cross country: Maximum both ends (90-100%). Drag: Minimum front (~0%), slight rear rake (~30%). Drift: Moderate (40-60%).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Minimum (aero efficiency). Drag: Low front, moderate rear (launch stability). Drift: Moderate (balance). Rally: Moderate-high (clearance). Off-road: Maximum (rock avoidance). Touge: Low (aero / handling).
categories.edgeCases: Lower ride height increases aero efficiency but risks grounding on bumps. Drag racing requires very low front for weight transfer. Off-road/rally need height for clearance over obstacles. Aero setups benefit from lower height (increased wing angle effectiveness). Tire width affects effective ride height.

categories.formula: Tire Pressure (PSI) = Baseline (compound-dependent) ± adjustments. Base pressures: Stock/Street 31.0 PSI, Sport 31.5 PSI, Semi-Slick 32.0 PSI, Race Slicks 32.5 PSI, Rally 29.0-30.0 PSI, Off-Road 28.0-30.0 PSI, Drift 20.0-26.0 PSI. Drivetrain adjustment: RWD (Front +0.5 to +1.0 PSI vs rear), FWD (Front +1.0 to +2.0 PSI vs rear), AWD (equal or front +0.2-0.5 PSI). Weight adjustment: per 600kg increment, add ~1.0-2.0 PSI. Width adjustment: +0.5-1.0 PSI per tire upgrade.
categories.concreteNumbers: Stock/Street: 31.0 PSI. Sport: 31.5 PSI. Semi-Slick: 32.0 PSI. Race Slicks: 32.5 PSI. Rally: 29-30 PSI. Off-Road: 28-30 PSI. Drift: 20-26 PSI (both ends low, rear often same or slightly lower). Weight example: 600kg kei car = 28-30 PSI on sport; 1800kg muscle car = 33-35 PSI. Adjustments in 0.5 PSI increments.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 32-32.5 PSI (slicks), baseline +0.5-1.0 front bias (RWD). Drag: Maximum front, minimum rear. Drift: 20-26 PSI (both soft). Rally: 29-30 PSI. Off-Road: 28-30 PSI. Street: 31-31.5 PSI.
categories.edgeCases: Weight heavily affects pressure: light cars run 28-30 PSI, heavy cars 33-35 PSI. Drift uses unusually low (20-26 PSI) for grip feel. Off-road needs lower for contact patch. Wider tire upgrades require +0.5-1.0 PSI to maintain shape stability. Track temperature affects hot pressure; all listed values are 'hot' targets. RWD benefits from front-biased pressure (turn-in). FWD needs high front (steering/power). AWD balanced (all four working equally).

categories.formula: Downforce (Front/Rear sliders) are direct percentages (0-100%). Weight Adjustment Formula (standard kits): Adjustment = (Car Weight - 2700lb) / 100lb. New Downforce = Max Downforce ± Adjustment. Balanced Rear Downforce = Base Rear + (Front Weight % - 47%) × 1.866667 lb.
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance: 0.50 = neutral, <0.50 = front-biased, >0.50 = rear-biased. Efficiency targets: >0.85 = excellent DF/drag ratio, 0.70-0.85 = average, <0.70 = poor. Example balanced setup: car with 64% front weight, base rear 137lb = 137 + (64-47)×1.87 ≈ 169lb rear. AWD road race: Max front, minimum rear (0.40-0.45 balance). RWD road race: Front 60-80%, Rear 60-90% (0.50-0.55 balance). FWD road race: High front, low-moderate rear (0.45-0.55 balance).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Max front downforce, tune rear for balance (0.45-0.55). Drag: Minimum both (near 0%, reduce drag). Drift: Race bumper only (slight front DF), skip rear wing. Rally: Minimal aero (speeds rarely exceed 150 kph). Cross-country: wasted tuning. Off-road: minimal. Street/commute: optional (low impact).
categories.edgeCases: AWD naturally has good rear traction, so minimum rear DF often correct (counterintuitive). RWD needs balanced DF (0.50-0.55). FWD prefers front bias (0.45-0.55). Drag racing must minimize DF (fuel economy). Drift requires minimal rear wing (rotation freedom). Aero only generates force at speed; low-speed corners (under 100 kph) = wasted PI points. Weight class affects max DF available; light cars = lower max DF.

categories.formula: Brake Balance = Front bias % (0-100%, where 50% = equal). No explicit formula; depends on weight distribution and drivetrain. Brake Pressure = % of maximum force (typical 70-200%, default 100%). Balance targets by drivetrain: RWD 50-55% front (forward weight transfer), AWD 52-56% front, FWD 55-62% front (front has more grip).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: 50-55% front bias (stable grip 52-55%, aggressive 48-51%). AWD: 52-56% front. FWD: 55-62% front (more weight on front). Drift: 55-70% front (for tandem stability). Pressure: default 100%, aggressive 105-115%, soft 85-95%.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: RWD 52-55% front, AWD 52-56%, FWD 55-62% (tuned for balance). Drag: front-heavy bias (60-70%) for weight transfer. Drift: 55-70% front (stability in tandems). Rally: moderate 50-55%. Touge: RWD 50-55%, standard approach.
categories.edgeCases: RWD needs slight front bias to match forward weight transfer under braking. FWD needs higher front bias (front already has more weight). AWD balanced between RWD and FWD. Dirt tracks may need less front bias (grip reduction). Drift tandem may use 60-70% front for rear stability. Pressure tuning: aggressive (105-115%) for better response, soft (85-95%) for modulation.

reliability: High for Forza 7 mechanics; partial for FH6 specifics. Formulas are from QuickTune calculator (legacy-established across FM7-FH6) and community consensus sources.

categories.formula: Final Drive = 4.25 + ((400 - Current_HP) / 6 × 0.01) OR Final Drive = Stock_FD + ((Stock_HP - Current_HP) / 6 × 0.01) for custom gearboxes
categories.concreteNumbers: For every 6hp change, adjust final drive by ±0.01. Example: 325hp car from 400hp reference = (400-325)/6 × 0.01 = 0.125, so 4.25 + 0.125 = 4.38 FD. Special cases: ≥800hp halve power; ≤200hp double power before calculating.
categories.goalVariation: Drag (maximize accel): Shorten 1st gear, maximize final drive for launch. Circuit (balanced): Use reference formula. Drift (long gears): Tune main drift gear to hit redline from dead stop. Rally/Touge: Shorter gearing for corner exit punch. Expressway/highway: Longer gearing for sustained pull.
categories.edgeCases: High-performance race cars without engine upgrades: reduce stock FD by 0.75. FWD/AWD may need slight adjustments but use same formula base. Reference values shift for premium gearboxes (e.g., race gearbox 2.89, 1.99, 1.49, 1.19, 0.94, 0.78).

categories.formula: Tire Pressure Base (compound-dependent): Stock/Street 28.0 psi, Sport 28.5 psi, Race 29.0 psi, Drag 29.5 psi. Adjustments: Open-wheel Front -0.5 to -4.0, Rear +0.5 to +4.0; AWD Front -1.0, Rear +1.0; FWD Front -2.0, Rear +2.0; Mid-engine Front +1.0, Rear -1.0; Rear-engine Front +2.0, Rear -2.0. High-performance race cars +0.5 psi additional.
categories.concreteNumbers: Stock/Street 28.0 PSI, Sport 28.5 PSI, Race 29.0 PSI, Drag 29.5 PSI. Semi-slicks ~32 PSI. Softer compounds require higher pressure (Sport/Race ~32 PSI vs Stock 26-28 PSI). High-Performance/Race/Prototype +0.5 PSI.
categories.goalVariation: Drag (max launch): 29.5+ PSI, maximize contact patch. Circuit (balanced grip): 29.0-29.5 PSI. Off-road/Rally: 1.9-2.0 BAR (~27-29 PSI) for flex. Drift: Lower pressure for sidewall compliance.
categories.edgeCases: Lower pressure = more grip but slower steering; higher pressure = sharper/faster but less cornering grip. Softer compounds (Sport/Race) need higher pressure for rigidity. Contact patch optimization crucial for grip ceiling.

categories.formula: Camber (FR reference): Utility/Street -3.0 to 0.0°, Sports -3.0 to 0.0°, High Perf -2.5 to -1.0°, Race -2.5 to -1.5°, GP Race -3.0 to -0.5°. Adjustments: FWD cars Front -0.4°, AWD cars Front/Rear -0.2°. Toe (most cars): Front 0.0°, Rear 0.0° (except older cars 0.0 to -0.3°). Caster: Utility/Street 5.0-7.0°, Race 4.0-5.0°, Prototype 6.0°, GP Race 7.0°.
categories.concreteNumbers: Circuit road race FR: Front -1.0° to -2.0°, Rear -0.5° to -1.0° (use -2.0° for slicks, -1.0° for sport tires). Camber adjustments per drivetrain: FWD -0.4° front, AWD -0.2° front/rear. Caster 4.0-7.0° range per class.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (grip): Negative camber per class ranges, balanced with tire temps. Drag (straight-line): Minimal camber, near zero for straight-line acceleration grip. Drift (angle): Moderate negative (-1.0 to -1.5°) for drifting. Off-road/Rally: -0.8° to -1.2° front, -0.5° to -0.8° rear for flat contact patches on dirt.
categories.edgeCases: More negative = more cornering grip but less braking/accel grip. Monitor tire temps: outside edge hot = more negative needed; inside hot = move toward zero. FWD needs more rear camber; RWD more front; AWD balanced. Tire width changes proportionally reduce camber.

categories.formula: Base ARB Stiffness = (Weight / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Distribution: RWD 1 unit per 1% weight distribution difference from 50%, FWD -1 per 1% difference (reversed), AWD 0.66 per 1% difference. Base stiffness ranges: Street/Utility 63-66%, Sports 61-65%, High Perf 40-55%, Race 35-62%, Prototype 80-89%, GP Race 84-90%.
categories.concreteNumbers: Example: Street car 2500lb @ 63% stiffness: (2500/2) / (200-126) = 16.89 ARB. RWD distribution rule: 1 per 1% from 50% (e.g., 51% FWD = Front ARB 1 higher). Base ranges: Street 63-66%, Sports 61-65%, Race 35-62%, GP 84-90%.
categories.goalVariation: Drag (launch stability): Higher front, lower rear for traction. Circuit (balanced): Match weight distribution 1:1 for RWD. Drift (oversteer): Soft rear ARB for rotation. Off-road: Softer overall for compliance.
categories.edgeCases: Sway bar diameter +1mm = ~15% spring rate change. Springs and dampers also affect balance during turning, so ARB is not strict 1:1 with weight distribution. FWD reverses distribution (front gets less ARB with front-heavy setup).

categories.formula: Base Spring Calculation = ((Max - Min) × Weight%) + Min, distributed by car type. Example RWD 3000lb @ 52% WD, Sports Car (~87%): Front = 3000/2 × 52% × 87% = 679 lb/in. Adjustments: Tire width +10" = Springs +0.5%, Downforce +10lb front = +0.5 spring rate. Chassis reinforcement Sport -2.75% front, Race -5.5% front.
categories.concreteNumbers: Front 83-104%, Rear 10-90% (car-type dependent). Weight distribution impact: ±4 kgf/mm per 1% shift from 50/50. Per 100lb weight change: spring adjustments proportional. Minimum ride heights: Street 4.0-6.0", High Perf 3.0-4.0", Race 3.0-5.0", Prototype 2.5-3.5", GP Race 2.5-4.5".
categories.goalVariation: Drag (soft front): Reduce front springs for weight transfer/launch. Circuit (balanced): Match weight distribution. Drift (rear-biased): Soft rear for rotation. Rally (compliance): Higher ride height (70-80% slider), softer springs for bump absorption.
categories.edgeCases: Heavier cars need stiffer springs. Front-heavy cars: add springs to front, subtract from rear (~40 kgf/mm per 10% WD imbalance). Downforce couples with springs (more aero = stiffer needed). Minimum ride height enforced by suspension travel limits.

categories.formula: Keep ride height as low as possible without bottoming out (car-type minimum enforced). Keep front/rear sliders aligned except for track-specific tuning. Minimums: Street/Sports 4.0-6.0", High Perf 3.0-4.0", Race 3.0-5.0", Prototype 2.5-3.5", GP Race 2.5-4.5".
categories.concreteNumbers: Road race: Minimum both ends. Rally: 70-80% slider range. Cross-country: Maximum both ends. General: Lowest possible without suspension bottoming.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (low CG): Minimum both ends. Rally (clearance): 70-80% slider range. Off-road/Cross-country (jumps): Maximum both ends. Drag (stability): Balanced or slightly raked.
categories.edgeCases: Lower = better CG and cornering, higher = more bump compliance. Unaligned front/rear creates artificial over/understeer. Car type minimum ride height is hard limit (cannot go below without suspension damage simulation).

categories.formula: Bump should be 30-55% of Rebound (targeting ~40%). Formula: Bump ÷ 0.4 = Rebound, OR Rebound = 1.5x to 1.8x Bump. Front base ranges FR: Rebound 6.0-9.5, Bump 3.5-6.5 (car-type dependent). Front-Rear offset by spring rate differential: <-10% diff = Rear +0.4 reb/+0.2 bump; -5% to 1.5% = Rear -0.2 reb/-0.1 bump; 36-40% = Rear -0.6 reb/-0.4 bump; >40% = Rear -1.2 reb/-0.8 bump. Per 100lb front weight change: ±0.1 inverse rebound/bump.
categories.concreteNumbers: 40% rule: Bump ~40% of Rebound (range 30-55% acceptable). Example: Bump 6.0 → Rebound ~15.0. FWD +2.5 front rebound/bump, AWD +1.5 front reb/bump, Prototype/GP +3.5 rear reb/bump. Front base (Rebound 6.0-9.5, Bump 3.5-6.5) adjusted per spring rate mismatch.
categories.goalVariation: Softer (30% ratio): Grippier, more chassis dive, underdamped feel. Stiffer (55% ratio): Firmer, responsive, less body motion. Drift (responsive): Higher compression for quick transitions. Off-road (compliance): Softer for bump absorption.
categories.edgeCases: Bump = compression (absorb hit), Rebound = extension (control return). Spring stiffness determines baseline damping needs. FWD/AWD need more front damping. Weight distribution changes bump/rebound offset.

categories.formula: Base distribution by car type: Street/Sports 48%, High Perf 44%, GP Race 52%. Base pressure: Street/Sports 125%, High Perf/Race 145%, GP Race 125%. Adjustments: FWD +4% distribution/-10% pressure, AWD +2%/-5%, Mid-engine -2%/+5%, Rear-engine -4%/+10%, Open-wheel +4-8%/-10-20%. Weight-based rule: Front weight % - 50 = brake bias offset (e.g., 43% front = 43-50=-7, divide by 2 = -3.5, subtract 3-4% from 50% baseline).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD 50-55% front, AWD 52-56%, FWD 55-62%. Base ranges: Street 48%, High Perf 44%, GP 52%. Pressure: 125-145% depending on class. Weight formula: 43% front car → 47-46% brake bias (subtract 3-4%).
categories.goalVariation: Drag (traction): Higher rear bias for stability. Circuit (balanced): Match weight distribution + 2-3%. Rally (landing): Rear-biased (48%) for jump landings. Drift: Rear-biased for entry stability.
categories.edgeCases: Forward weight transfer under braking requires front bias. FWD has more front weight, needs more front bias. Rear-engine cars shift weight rearward under braking (less front bias). Open-wheel very front-biased.

categories.formula: RWD: Road 40-65% accel / 15-30% decel; Drift 80-100% accel / varies; Drag maximize accel. FWD: Apply RWD logic to front (40-65% accel), but higher accel restricts turn-in. AWD three-zone: Front, Rear, Center Balance (displays rear % torque, 50%=equal, >65% common for road). Road AWD: Front 28-40% accel/0-15% decel, Rear 55-100% accel/20-45% decel, Center 70-85% rear. Dirt AWD: Front 30-40%/0-15%, Rear 55-70%/20-25%, Center 65-75%. Cross AWD: Front 35-45%/5-15%, Rear 65-75%/20-25%, Center 55-65%.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD road: 40-65% accel, 15-30% decel. RWD drift: 80-100% accel (welded-diff behavior). RWD drag: maximize accel. AWD road: Front 28%, Rear 100%, Center 70-85% rear. Accel increments in 2% only. Hard limits: FWD/AWD accel <95%, decel >5%.
categories.goalVariation: Drag (launch): Maximize rear accel lock. Circuit (balance): RWD 55% accel/15% decel. Drift (angle): RWD 90-100% accel for welded feel. Rally/Touge: RWD 50-60% accel. Off-road/Cross: Lower accel lock (35-45% front) for traction breaking.
categories.edgeCases: Accel locks diff under throttle (more oversteer RWD, understeer FWD). Decel locks off-throttle/braking (stability into corners). Center Balance >65% rear common for road (understeer prevention). Shorter wheelbase 70-80%, longer up to 90%. No front decel on road-race AWD (rarely need it).

categories.formula: Standard Forza Aero: Downforce = Max_Downforce + ((3000 - Current_Weight) / 100). Example 2900lb FWD with 100 max front/75 max rear: Front 100+2=102 (capped), Rear 75+2=77. Custom aero: Downforce = Max + ((Stock_Weight - 300 - Current_Weight) / 100). High power (≥800hp) multiplier 1.5×. Low power (≤200hp) Front ×0.7, Rear ×0.4. Aero Balance adjustment: Per 1% weight distribution deviation from 47% = ±1.87 lb rear downforce.
categories.concreteNumbers: Weight base 3000lb reference. 100lb car weight difference = 1 lb downforce change. 800+ hp cars: 1.5× multiplier. <200hp cars: Front ×0.7, Rear ×0.4. Aero balance 0.40-0.45 window preferred for balance. Per 1% WD from 47%: ±1.87 rear downforce.
categories.goalVariation: Drag (low aero): Minimize downforce/drag trade-off. Circuit (high grip): Balance front/rear in 0.40-0.45 zone. Drift (low aero): Reduce downforce. Highway/Expressway (low drag): Minimize aero.
categories.edgeCases: High power cars (800+hp): Need 1.5× standard downforce. Low power (<200hp): Reduced effectiveness (front ×0.7, rear ×0.4). Weight-sensitive: heavier cars need more downforce; lighter less. Aero balance critical—imbalanced setups cause understeer/oversteer separate from suspension.

reliability: High for FH6-specific concrete numbers and ranges. Damping formulas tested in community; spring formulas from decompiled ForzaTune Pro source (TuneLab). Tire pressures verified against multiple guides. No mathematical derivations provided for all categories but empirical ranges well-documented.

categories.formula: Front Bump = Min Bump + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1; Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6; Bump should be 40-70% of Rebound (baseline ~60%)
categories.concreteNumbers: Sports class min bump: 4.4-4.8; High Performance: 4.5-4.9; Race Car: 4.7-4.9. Rear damping offset by spring difference: 0-1.5% diff → Rebound -0.2/Bump -0.1; 1.5-35% → -0.3/-0.2; 36-40% → -0.6/-0.4; >40% → -1.2/-0.8
categories.goalVariation: Rally/Off-road: min bump set to 1.0 (softest) for terrain absorption. Road/Circuit: standard formula. Drift: higher bump values (~6-8) to absorb transitions
categories.edgeCases: Rally/off-road min bump overrides class minimum; FWD and AWD may need different front/rear balance than RWD

categories.formula: Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Slider Position %) + Min Rate; Spring Rate = M × (2πf)² where f = 6.79e-7 × (PI-100)² + 2.45
categories.concreteNumbers: Weight distribution adjustment: shift ~4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50. Sports class slider ranges: Front 87-98%, Rear 58-80%. High Performance: Front 85-93%, Rear 63-84%. Race Car: Front 83-93%, Rear 59-85%. Frequency polynomial: base ~2.45 Hz, adjusted by (PI-100) squared term
categories.goalVariation: Sports/High Performance: moderate spring ranges. Race Car: stiffer. Rally: softer front. Drag: soft front for traction, stiff rear for stability. Drift: soft for weight transfer
categories.edgeCases: FWD uses opposite slider distribution vs RWD/AWD (higher rear%, lower front%). AWD: adjust ~0.66 per 1% weight vs 1.0 for RWD

categories.formula: Base = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%)
categories.concreteNumbers: Class stiffness targets: Sports 61-65%, High Performance 40-46%, Race Car 35-62%. Front/Rear ARB adjustment: RWD +1 per 1% front weight above 50%; FWD -1 per 1%; AWD +0.66 per 1%. Typical starting ranges: RWD Front 18-25/Rear 25-35; AWD Front 22-30/Rear 28-38; FWD Front 8-15/Rear 25-40
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: stiff settings for flat cornering. Rally: softer for body movement. Drift: very soft front, stiff rear for rotation. Off-road: very soft both ends
categories.edgeCases: FWD inverts the normal distribution (lower front stiffness). AWD center differential affects both axles

categories.formula: No explicit formula; empirical ranges by discipline and tire grip level
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber: Road racing Front -1.0° to -2.0°/Rear -0.5° to -1.0° (slick tires: -2.0°, sport: -1.0°). Rally Front -0.8° to -1.2°/Rear -0.5° to -0.8°. Cross country Front/Rear -0.5°. Drift Front -3.0° to -5.0°/Rear -1.0°. Caster 5.0-7.0° (light cars 5.0-5.5°, mid-weight 5.5-6.5°, heavy/high-speed 6.5-7.0°). Toe: 0.0° default; minimal deviation due to scrub penalty
categories.goalVariation: Circuit racing: high negative camber. Rally: moderate negative. Off-road: minimal camber. Drift: extreme negative front camber. Drag: minimal camber for straight-line grip
categories.edgeCases: Slick tires support more negative camber than sport tires. Heavier cars benefit from higher caster. FWD may need different toe strategy for steering feel

categories.formula: Pressure = Base + Adjustment; A 600 kg car wants ~3 PSI less than 1800 kg car on same compound; Wider tires: +0.5-1.0 PSI
categories.concreteNumbers: Stock/Street 31.0 PSI; Sport 31.5 PSI; Semi-Slick 32.0 PSI; Race Slicks 32.5 PSI; Rally 29.0-30.0 PSI; Off-road 28.0-30.0 PSI; Drift 20.0-26.0 PSI. Adjust by 0.5 PSI at a time. Example: 600kg kei car on sport tires 28-30 PSI; 1800 kg muscle car same compound 33-35 PSI. Drag: ~25 PSI rear, 32 PSI front
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 31-33 PSI typical. Rally: 29-30 PSI. Off-road: 28-30 PSI. Drift: 20-26 PSI (intentionally low). Drag: front-biased pressure split
categories.edgeCases: Drift requires unusually low pressure (high slip). Off-road varies with terrain type. Width: 235→285mm tires add 0.5-1.0 PSI

categories.formula: Brake Bias % = front brakes / (front brakes + rear brakes) × 100; No explicit front-to-rear ratio formula given
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD 50-55% front. AWD 52-56% front. FWD 55-62% front. Adjustments: understeer on turn-in → increase front bias. Oversteer on trail brake → decrease front bias (+1-2% rear)
categories.goalVariation: Road racing: 50-56% typical. Drift: 45-50% (rear-biased). Drag: front-biased for stability on power. Rally: similar to road racing
categories.edgeCases: FH5 and earlier had inverted brake bias slider (bug fixed in FH6). Trail braking may require +1-2% rear bias for corner stability

categories.formula: No explicit formula; empirical lock percentages by type and phase
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD Road Racing: Acceleration 40-65%, Deceleration 15-30%. RWD Drift: Acceleration 80-100% (near-welded). AWD Road Racing: Front 45% accel/0% decel, Rear 65% accel/15% decel. AWD Center Balance: Road 70-85% rear bias (meaning % torque to rear); Rally 65-75%; Cross-country 55-65%
categories.goalVariation: Circuit road racing: moderate accel lock (40-65% RWD), low decel (15-30%). Drift: extreme accel lock (80-100%). Rally: mixed (65-75% AWD center). Off-road: variable terrain dependent
categories.edgeCases: AWD has 3 differentials (front, rear, center); center balance displayed as % rear torque. FWD accel lock reduces wheel spin on power. Deceleration lock too high causes lift-off understeer; too low causes lift-off oversteer

categories.formula: Wheel RPM = Engine RPM / (Gear Ratio × Final Drive); V (km/h) = [RPM / (Gear × FD)] × (π × Tire Diameter mm / 1000) × (60 / 1000); Suggested FDR = (Max Engine RPM / (Target Top Speed km/h × Top Gear)) × (π × Tire Diameter mm / 1000) × (60 / 1000)
categories.concreteNumbers: No specific value ranges provided in sources; calculated per-vehicle. Higher Final Drive = shorter gears (better accel, worse top speed). Power band optimization: keep engine RPM in sweet spot across gear changes
categories.goalVariation: Drag: maximize acceleration, ignore top speed (high FD). Circuit: balance accel and top speed per track. Drag: highest FD possible. Rally: varies by stage length. Touge: moderate FD for acceleration consistency
categories.edgeCases: Tire diameter affects all calculations directly. Supercharged/turbocharged engines may have different power band peaks. Max engine RPM varies by tune

categories.formula: Downforce scales with wing angle and vehicle speed (no explicit formula given); Aero Balance = Front Downforce / (Front + Rear Downforce)
categories.concreteNumbers: AWD: Maximum front downforce, Minimum rear downforce. RWD: 60-80% slider front, 60-90% slider rear. FWD: High to maximum front, Low to moderate rear. Aero Balance targets: Neutral 0.50; Front-biased (AWD road) 0.40-0.45; Neutral-to-rear (RWD) 0.50-0.55; Slightly front (FWD/Drift) 0.45-0.55. Efficiency benchmarks: Excellent >0.85, Average 0.70-0.85, Poor <0.70
categories.goalVariation: Circuit high-speed: rear downforce matters. Drag: minimal aero (all drag, no benefit). Rally: low aero. Cross-country: minimal aero. Drift: balanced front bias. Touge: moderate front-to-rear balance
categories.edgeCases: AWD inherent understeer compensated by max front aero. Downforce only effective at speed (negligible <80 km/h). FWD front handles steering+power+braking, needs front downforce. RWD can run more rear balanced

categories.formula: No explicit formula; suspension telemetry must not max out during driving. Increase ride height if telemetry bar reaches maximum
categories.concreteNumbers: Road Racing: Minimum both ends (lowest CG). Dirt Rally: 70-80% slider range. Cross Country: Maximum both ends (clearance). Drag: Minimum front, slightly higher rear (rake forward). Drift: Minimum both ends
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: minimize for CG advantage. Off-road: maximize for terrain clearance. Drag: front rake for traction. Rally: moderate for debris clearance while maintaining CG
categories.edgeCases: Bottom-out detection via telemetry bar graph; adjust springs if ride height already maxed. Drag racing may use intentional front rake (weight transfer)

source: ForzaTune (forzatune.com), Forza.guide, Dialed Racing, ForzaFire Platform & Handling Guide, Official Forza Forums, DRTuned Racing
reliability: High for formulas and ranges; concrete per-car/per-mode numbers are minimal. Most sources provide starting-point baselines requiring track-specific refinement.

categories.formula: Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Weight Distribution %) + Min Rate. Front Bump = Min Bump + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6 (bump is 60% of rebound). For frequency approach: K = (Hz² × M) / 19.56 (imperial) where K = spring rate, M = weight over axle in lbs.
categories.concreteNumbers: Min Spring Rates (lbs/in): 138.3-1279.8. Metric: 40.1-239.7 KGF/MM. Frequency targets: OEM cars 0.5-1.0Hz, lowering 1.0-1.5Hz, rally 1.5-2.0Hz, moderate aero 2.5-3.5Hz, high aero 3.5-5.0+Hz. Frequency multipliers (imperial): 1.21Hz=0.10, 1.98Hz=0.20, 2.42Hz=0.30, 2.80Hz=0.40, 3.13Hz=0.50, 3.43Hz=0.60. Weight distribution adjustment: shift ~4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 85-98% front (Sports), 58-80% rear. Drag: rear stiff / front soft for weight transfer. Rally: 70-80% ride height, softer springs. Drift: 50-75% bump stiffness (start 50%, adjust up). Off-Road: raise ride height max.
categories.edgeCases: FWD swaps front/rear percentages. Race Springs unlock full suspension tuning; without upgrade, alignment/spring/damping locked at factory. Bump = 40-70% of Rebound depending on class.

categories.formula: Bump = 40-70% of Rebound, target ~60% baseline. Front Bump = Min Bump (class-dependent) + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6. Rear offset determined by spring rate difference: 0-1.5% diff = rear -0.2 rebound/-0.1 bump; 1.5-35% = -0.3 rebound/-0.2 bump; 36-40% = -0.6 rebound/-0.4 bump; >40% = -1.2 rebound/-0.8 bump.
categories.concreteNumbers: Min Bump by class: Sports 4.4-4.8, High Performance 4.5-4.9, Race Car 4.7-4.9. Rally/Off-Road: add +1.0 to rebound. Typical range: bump 5-8, rebound 8-14.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: 60% bump/rebound ratio, track-specific. Drag: aggressive damping for weight transfer. Rally: +1.0 rebound to handle terrain. Drift: softer damping for predictability. Off-Road: higher rebound for compliance.
categories.edgeCases: Heavier cars need higher base values. Stiffer chassis requires higher rebound. Unsprung mass estimation needed for precision.

categories.formula: No explicit formula provided. Camber, toe, caster are independent sliders tuned to target negative camber at dynamic apex around 0° for max grip. Caster typically 5.0-7.0°; toe defaults 0.0° both ends; camber negative for cornering.
categories.concreteNumbers: Circuit: camber front -1.0° to -2.0°, rear -0.5° to -1.0° (higher for slicks, lower for sport). Rally: front -0.8° to -1.2°, rear -0.5° to -0.8°. Drift: front -3.0° to -5.0°, rear -1.0°. Cross-country: front/rear -0.5°. Caster: light/agile 5.0-5.5°, mid-road 5.5-6.5°, heavy/high-speed 6.5-7.0°. Toe: slight front toe-out -0.1° to -0.2°, rear toe-in +0.1° to +0.3° (causes tire scrub).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: moderate negative camber. Drift: aggressive front camber (-5.0°) with front toe-out (+1.0°). Rally: less aggressive. Drag: neutral alignment. Off-Road: minimal camber for flat contact patch.
categories.edgeCases: Dynamic camber varies with suspension flex (older cars more flex, modern cars more rigid). Static camber set so dynamic camber ≈ 0° at corner apex. Toe increases tire wear; minimize where possible.

categories.formula: Base = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Distribution by drivetrain: RWD (+1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%), AWD (+0.66 per 1%), FWD (-1 per 1%). Alternative: (Max Rate - Min Rate) × Weight Distribution % + Min Rate.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD front 18-25, rear 25-35. AWD front 22-30, rear 28-38. FWD front 8-15, rear 25-40. Example (1,400kg AWD, 60% front, High Performance 43%): Base=13.5, Front=20.1, Rear=6.9. Mechanical Balance target: 0.55-0.65 (sweet spot 0.60).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: balance toward 0.60 mechanical balance. Drift: front soft/rear stiff for rotation. Rally: softer ARBs overall. Drag: stiff rear. Aero-heavy: maxed ARBs for chassis preservation.
categories.edgeCases: Max out both ARBs first as baseline, soften toward target. Stiff bars sharpen response and preserve alignment under load. Springs and dampers also affect balance.

categories.formula: No explicit formula; ride height is a slider. Raising RH increases braking/accel capability but raises center of mass (hurts turning). Optimal: lowest possible without violating car-type minimum.
categories.concreteNumbers: Circuit: minimum both ends. Rally: 70-80% slider range. Cross-country: maximum both ends. Drag: minimum front, slightly higher rear (rake). Drift: minimum both ends.
categories.goalVariation: High-speed/circuit: minimize. Off-road: maximize. Drag: rake setup (front low, rear higher). Aero-heavy: raise slightly to maintain aero angle.
categories.edgeCases: Car-type minimum not tunable; suspension upgrade status determines full range access.

categories.formula: No explicit formula; pressure varies by compound and weight. General principle: lower pressure = larger contact patch (more grip, softer ride, faster heat). Higher pressure = smaller patch (less peak grip, firmer, slower heat).
categories.concreteNumbers: Stock/Street 31.0 PSI, Sport 31.5 PSI, Semi-Slick 32.0 PSI, Race Slicks 32.5 PSI, Rally 29-30 PSI, Off-Road 28-30 PSI, Drift 20-26 PSI. Weight adjustment: 600kg kei car 28-30 PSI sport, 1,800kg muscle 33-35 PSI same compound. Road race slicks 31.5-32.5 PSI (front slightly higher). Drag: ~25 PSI rear, 32 PSI front. Cross-country: 1.9 BAR (~27.5 PSI).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit slicks: 31.5-32.5 PSI. Drift: 20-22 PSI both ends. Rally: 22-27 PSI. Drag: asymmetric (32F/25R). Off-Road: 27-30 PSI.
categories.edgeCases: FWD: front 1-2 PSI higher than rear. RWD: front 0.5-1.0 PSI higher. AWD: symmetric or near-equal. Grippier tires (slicks) need more pressure due to stiffer sidewalls.

categories.formula: Speed = (RPM × Tire Circumference) / (Overall Gear Ratio × 60). Final Drive for 6-speed default: (400 - Car HP) / 600 + 4.25. Custom transmission: (Stock HP - Current HP) / 600 + Stock FD. Power series model: Gear Ratio = A × n^B (starting A=3.00, B=-0.65). Top Speed per gear: (Top Speed × Last Gear Ratio) / Target Gear Ratio.
categories.concreteNumbers: Suggested FDR = (Max RPM / (Target Speed km/h × Top Gear Ratio)) × (π × Tire Diameter mm / 1000) × (60/1000). Power series example produces: 3.00, 1.91, 1.47, 1.22, 1.05, 0.94 (starting FD 3.75). Frequency values (imperial): 1.21Hz→0.10, 1.98Hz→0.20, 2.42Hz→0.30, 2.80Hz→0.40, 3.13Hz→0.50, 3.43Hz→0.60.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: redline in top gear near max track speed. Drag: high FD (short gears, quicker accel, lower top speed). Top Speed: low FD (long gears, minimal aero). Rally: gears for mid-range torque.
categories.edgeCases: Redline in top gear at your target top speed. Engine upgrades change max RPM (recalculate). Tire size changes affect speed calculations. Weight affects acceleration feel but not ratio math.

categories.formula: No explicit formula provided. Acceleration lock % = throttle-on traction behavior (higher = both wheels equal speed, massive drive, induces understeer). Deceleration/Coast lock % = braking/coasting behavior.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD acceleration lock: 40-65% for road racing (no universal formula). No concrete decel/coast percentages published. General principle: higher accel lock = oversteer reduction but turn-in reduction.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: moderate accel lock (40-50%) for stability. Drift: lower accel lock for rotation. Rally: balanced per terrain. Drag: high accel lock for straight-line traction.
categories.edgeCases: Deceleration only functions off-throttle (during coasting/braking); game description may be inaccurate. AWD benefits from lower accel lock than RWD due to inherent traction.

categories.formula: Brake Bias is a slider: 0-100%, where 100% = front only, 0% = rear only, 50% = default balanced. No weight-distribution formula provided; tuners use starting points and adjust. General principle: weight shifts forward under braking.
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: 50-55% front bias. AWD: 52-56%. FWD: 55-62%. Drift/Dirt: 45-50%. Brake Pressure: 100% default, 105-115% for heavy cars, 85-95% for light cars. Starting points by weight: 52% RWD (52% front weight), 54% mid/rear-engine cars (42% front weight). Conservative: 43-47% front. Aggressive (ABS on): 40-45% front.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit trail-braking: 53-54% rear bias (more rotation). Drag: minimal braking focus. Off-Road: softer bias (45-50%).
categories.edgeCases: Repeated emphasis: these are starting points; individual cars require testing. Rear lock-up more likely if bias too rear-heavy. ABS and driving style (early vs. trail-brake) affect tuning direction.

categories.formula: General physics (not Forza-specific): Downforce = 1/2 × Air Density × Wing Area × Lift Coefficient × Velocity². Forza uses front splitter & rear wing angle sliders; more angle = more downforce + more drag.
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance (front/total ratio) targets: AWD road racing 0.40-0.45, RWD 0.50-0.55, FWD 0.45-0.55, Drift 0.45-0.50. Efficiency benchmarks: >0.85 excellent, 0.70-0.85 average, <0.70 poor. Slider position: AWD road = max front / min rear, RWD road = 60-80% front / 60-90% rear, FWD road = high-max front / low-mod rear. Drag: both sliders ~zero.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit high-speed: maximize downforce. Top Speed runs: minimize aero (zero if possible). Drag: zero/floor both. Rally: low-moderate aero. Drift: 0.45-0.50 balance.
categories.edgeCases: Rear wing produces more drag per downforce unit than front splitter. Aero values are car-specific; no universal numbers. AWD needs less rear aero due to drivetrain traction.

source: https://www.forzafire.com/guides/forza-horizon-6-platform-and-handling-tuning-guide (primary source for spring, damper, ARB, brake, alignment formulas)
reliability: High — concrete formulas with specific numerical ranges; formulas are equation-based with example calculations provided. FH6-specific and validated across multiple tuning guides.

categories.formula: Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Slider Position %) + Min Rate. Adjust approximately 4 kgf/mm per 1% deviation from 50/50 weight distribution.
categories.goalVariation: Sports: Front 87-98% / Rear 58-80% (RWD/AWD, reversed for FWD); High Performance: Front 85-93% / Rear 63-84% (RWD/AWD, reversed for FWD); Race Car: Front 83-93% / Rear 59-85% (RWD/AWD, reversed for FWD)
categories.concreteNumbers: Sports class example: 4 kgf/mm adjustment per 1% weight distribution difference; slider range for front 83-98%, rear 58-85%

categories.formula: Front Bump = Minimum Bump (by class) + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1; Front Rebound = Front Bump ÷ 0.6; Bump should be 40-70% of Rebound, target ~60% baseline. Rear Damping Offsets: if spring difference 0-1.5% then Rear Rebound -0.2 and Bump -0.1; if 1.5-35% then Rear Rebound -0.3 and Bump -0.2; if 36-40% then Rear Rebound -0.6 and Bump -0.4; if above 40% then Rear Rebound -1.2 and Bump -0.8
categories.goalVariation: Sports/All classes prioritize stability; Drift prioritizes responsiveness with lower damping values
categories.concreteNumbers: Sports minimum bump: 4.4-4.8; High Performance: 4.5-4.9; Race Car: 4.7-4.9. Example: 1,400 kg car (3,086 lbs) at 60% front on Sports = Front Bump 4.6 + 0.93 = 5.5; Rebound = 9.2. Baseline range: Rebound 9-14, Bump 50-75% of rebound

categories.formula: Base = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). RWD distribution: +1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%. FWD distribution: -1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%. AWD distribution: +0.66 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%.
categories.goalVariation: Sports stiffness 61-65%; High Performance 40-46%; Race Car 35-62%. RWD typical 18-25 front / 25-35 rear; AWD typical 22-30 front / 28-38 rear; FWD typical 8-15 front / 25-40 rear
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: Front 18-25, Rear 25-35; AWD: Front 22-30, Rear 28-38; FWD: Front 8-15, Rear 25-40. Example: 1,400 kg (3,086 lbs) AWD at 60% front, High Performance (43% stiffness) = Base 13.5, with +6.6 front/-6.6 rear distribution = Front 20.1, Rear 6.9

categories.formula: Camber: varies by discipline. Toe: keep close to zero general rule. Caster: target 5.0-7.0 degrees with light cars 5.0-5.5, mid-weight road cars 5.5-6.5, heavy/high-speed 6.5-7.0
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: Front camber -1.0 to -2.0°, Rear -0.5 to -1.0°; Drift: Front -3.0 to -5.0°, Rear -1.0°; Dirt Rally: Front -0.8 to -1.2°, Rear -0.5 to -0.8°; Cross-country: Front/Rear -0.5° minimal; Drag: minimum both ends with slight rear rise
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber baselines: AWD front -1.5 to -2.0°, rear -1.0 to -1.5°. Caster 5.0-7.0° depending on weight class. Toe: front +0.1 to +0.3°, rear -0.1 to -0.3°

categories.formula: Adjust as percentage of slider range based on discipline. Slider positions vary by car.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: minimum both ends; Dirt Rally: 70-80% slider range; Cross-country: maximum both ends; Drag: minimum front, slightly higher rear
categories.concreteNumbers: Race cars 4-6, Street cars 5-7, Prototypes 3.5-4.5, Off-roaders 5-7. Road racing: minimum both ends; Dirt rally: 70-80% of slider range; Cross-country: maximum both ends

categories.formula: Pressure directly affects contact patch size and grip. Lower pressure = more grip/smoother load transfer. Higher pressure = sharper response/reduced grip ceiling.
categories.goalVariation: Stock/Street/Rally: 26-28 PSI; Semi-slicks/Slicks/Drift: 30-33 PSI; AWD starting: 28 PSI front, 27-28 PSI rear; Heavy vehicles: 32 PSI; Asphalt road racing: 1.8-1.9 bar cold / 2.1-2.2 bar hot; Dirt/Rally: 1.5-1.6 bar cold / 1.8-1.9 bar hot; Off-road: 1.2-1.4 bar cold / 1.4-1.6 bar hot
categories.concreteNumbers: AWD: 28 PSI front, 27-28 PSI rear; Heavy vehicles: 32 PSI; Racing: 30-33 PSI typical

categories.formula: Brake Bias = front-biased percentage. RWD: 50-55% front; AWD: 52-56% front; FWD: 55-62% front; Drift/Dirt: 45-50% (rear-biased). Brake Pressure: default 100%, range typically 70-200%
categories.goalVariation: Road racing (RWD): 50-55% front; Road racing (AWD): 52-56% front; Road racing (FWD): 55-62% front; Drift/Dirt: 45-50% rear bias
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD: 50-55% front bias; AWD: 52-56% front bias; FWD: 55-62% front bias; Drift/Dirt: 45-50% (rear-biased). Brake Pressure: 70-200% range (default 100%)

categories.formula: Acceleration and Deceleration percentages apply independently. RWD/FWD Road Racing: Acceleration 40-65%, Deceleration 15-30%. Drift: Acceleration 80-100% (near-welded). AWD Road Racing recommended: Front Accel 28% / Front Decel 0%; Rear Accel 100% / Rear Decel 45%; Center Balance 70-85% (rear bias)
categories.goalVariation: Circuit Road Racing RWD: Accel 45-65%, Decel 15-30%; Drift RWD: Accel 80-100%, Decel variable; AWD Road: Front Accel 28%, Rear Accel 100%, Center 70-85%; AWD Rally: Front Accel 30-40%/Rear 55-70%, Center 65-75%; AWD Cross-Country: Front Accel 35-45%/Rear 65-75%, Center 55-65%
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD Road: Accel 45-65%, Decel 15-30%; FWD: Accel 30-60%, Decel 5-15%; AWD Front: Accel 15-25%, Decel 5-15%; AWD Rear: Accel 45-75%, Decel 5-15%

categories.formula: Aero Balance stat target: 0.40-0.45 (0 = perfectly balanced; higher = front-heavy). Adjust front and rear downforce sliders until Aero Balance reads target. More downforce = more drag and speed loss.
categories.goalVariation: Time attack: near-max downforce with 0.40-0.45 balance; Sprint cars: much less downforce; Circuit balanced: 0.40-0.45 target; Drag racing: minimal aero required
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance target 0.40-0.45; more downforce increases drag; typical ranges provided as slider adjustments (no absolute kN values published)

categories.formula: Final Drive adjusts all gears uniformly: lower final drive = longer gears (higher top speed, slower acceleration); higher final drive = shorter gears (lower top speed, faster acceleration). No published formula; tune by visual feedback on speed graph.
categories.goalVariation: Drag: drag top gear to right, curve lower gears smoothly, iterate 1st gear on strip; Circuit: balance for track top speed vs. exit acceleration; Rally/Dirt: shorter gearing for loose surface traction; Touge: medium gearing for sustained power band
categories.concreteNumbers: 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10-speed transmission available for Race Transmission; PI cost increases with engine power; no specific numerical gear ratios (e.g., 3.5:1) are published by Forza formula

source: Multiple sources: ForzaTune (forzatune.com), ForzaFire (forzafire.com), forza.guide, FH6Guide (fh6guide.com), Forza Community Forums (forums.forza.net), SkyCoach, and additional tuning guides
reliability: High for tire pressure, camber/toe/caster, suspension damping, brake bias; Medium for gearing (community formulas), differential ranges; Low for aero (lacks specific drag coefficients); Most data from FH6 guides, some from FH5/FM tuning carried forward

categories.formula: Cold tire PSI set to warm target: cold pressure 27-30 PSI (1.90-2.07 bar), warm pressure target 32-34 PSI (2.20-2.35 bar). Adjustment by 0.5 PSI / 0.05 BAR increments. Weight-based rule: lighter vehicles (600 kg) 28-30 PSI on sport; heavier vehicles (1800 kg) 33-35 PSI on same compound.
categories.concreteNumbers: Stock/Street 31.0 PSI, Sport 31.5 PSI, Semi-Slick 32.0 PSI, Race Slicks 32.5 PSI, Rally 29-30 PSI, Off-Road 28-30 PSI, Drift 20-26 PSI. FWD: front +1.0-2.0 PSI over rear; RWD: front +0.5-1.0 PSI over rear; AWD: equal or front +0.2-0.5 PSI; Drift: both ends ~20 PSI, symmetric or rear slightly lower
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (31.5-32.5 PSI race compounds) > Rally/Off-Road (28-30 PSI) > Drift (20-26 PSI low pressure for predictable slides); Drag (32+ PSI for launch stability)
categories.edgeCases: Weight distribution heavily affects baseline—kei cars need lower pressures than muscle cars on identical compounds. RWD vs FWD split differs due to weight transfer characteristics. AWD more uniform. Drift uses extremely low pressures (1.38-1.79 bar) to maximize tire scrub and controllability.

categories.formula: Three community-published methods: (1) HP-based for 6-speed race box: FD = 4.25 + ((400 - HP) / 600); (2) Power series: Ratio = A × n^B where A = first gear, B = -0.65, recommended (3.00, 1.91, 1.47, 1.22, 1.05, 0.94) with FD 3.75; (3) Weight-based: FD = Stock FD + ((Weight/HP) × (1st Gear - Last Gear) / 100). General principle: raising final drive shortens all gears (better acceleration, lower top speed); lowering lengthens gears (worse acceleration, higher top speed). Target: make final gear barely hit top speed at end of longest straight. Keep engine in power band (max torque to max RPM).
categories.concreteNumbers: Race 6-speed default (2.89, 1.99, 1.49, 1.19, 0.94, 0.78). Recommended power series (3.00, 1.91, 1.47, 1.22, 1.05, 0.94) with FD 3.75. No universal drag/rally-specific ratios published; community formulas imply FD range 2.5-5.0 depending on HP and weight.
categories.goalVariation: Drag: maximize FD for lowest 0-100 mph time (shorter gears, higher RPM exits). Circuit: balanced FD targeting end-of-straight top speed. Rally/Off-Road: generally higher FD than circuit for exit acceleration. Drag-specific: first gear critical for launch traction.
categories.edgeCases: Custom race boxes allow full gear tuning; stock boxes limited to final drive only. Turbo/naturally aspirated engines may shift power band. AWD vs RWD weight distribution affects optimal ratios. Low-HP vehicles need higher FD multipliers; high-HP vehicles lower. No FH6-specific formula published—community relying on FH5/Motorsport math.

categories.formula: Camber: negative angle (e.g., -2.0° = wheel top tilts inward). No calculation formula; specific angle targets given per discipline. Toe: default 0.0°; non-zero causes tire scrub and speed loss. Small toe-out (0.1-0.3°) can sharpen turn-in. Caster: positive angle (5.0-7.0° typical range) increases straight-line stability and self-centering; higher caster = more stable on straights, higher steering effort.
categories.concreteNumbers: Camber: Road racing asphalt -1.0 to -2.0° front, -0.5 to -1.0° rear; Dirt rally -0.8 to -1.2° front, -0.5 to -0.8° rear; Cross-country -0.5° both; Drift -3.0 to -5.0° front, -1.0° rear. Toe: 0.0° standard; max toe-out ±0.1 to ±0.3° front; toe-in ±0.1 to ±0.3° rear. Caster: 5.0-5.5° (light/agile), 5.5-6.5° (mid-weight road race), 6.5-7.0° (heavy/high-speed); AWD starting ~-1.5 to -2.0° front, -1.0 to -1.5° rear.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Road race: Camber -1.5 to -2.0° (moderate negative). Drift: Camber -3.0 to -5.0° (aggressive negative for extreme lean). Off-Road: Camber -0.5 to -0.8° (minimal, maintains contact). Toe: near 0° for all except minor toe-out adjustments. Caster: 6.5-7.0° for high-speed stability (circuit/drag), lower (5.0-5.5°) for agility (drift).
categories.edgeCases: Camber changes when ride height adjusts—need re-tuning after ride height modifications. FWD vs RWD: front camber more critical on FWD (steering tires). Extreme drift camber (-4 to -5°) trades braking grip for slide responsiveness. Toe scrub costs ~1-2% top speed per 0.1° deviation from 0.0°.

categories.formula: Base ARB calculation: Base = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Then adjust for weight distribution and drivetrain: RWD +1 ARB per 1% weight above 50% front; FWD -1 ARB per 1% (very soft front); AWD +0.66 per 1%. General principle: stiffer front reduces understeer; stiffer rear reduces oversteer. Target ARB stiffness window 0.55-0.65 (sweet spot ~0.60). Below 0.55 understeer; above 0.65 instability.
categories.concreteNumbers: Example 1400 kg AWD, 60% front, High Performance class (43% stiffness): Base = 13.5, Front = 20.1, Rear = 6.9. Typical ranges: RWD Front 18-25 / Rear 25-35; AWD Front 22-30 / Rear 28-38; FWD Front 8-15 / Rear 25-40. Class stiffness % values: Sports 61-65%, High Performance 40-46%, Race Car 35-62%.
categories.goalVariation: Road race: ARB stiffness window 0.55-0.65. Drift: higher front ARB (stiffer) for weight transfer control. Off-Road: lower ARB (more compliance for rough terrain). Drag: moderate, balanced for launch stability.
categories.edgeCases: FWD front ARB must stay very soft (8-15) to preserve turn-in grip on steering tires. AWD center balance also influences ARB tuning perception. Weight distribution heavily impacts calculation—50/50 = base; each 1% deviation multiplied by drivetrain factor. Rally/off-road add +1.0 to rebound across board.

categories.formula: Primary formula: Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × WeightRatio %) + Min Rate. Alternative natural frequency method (imperial): K (lb/in) = (Hz² × M) / 19.56, or reverse Hz = √(K / M × 19.56). Simplified weight-based: Weight × Distribution % × Frequency Multiplier = Spring Rate. Adjustment rule: shift ~4 kgf/mm (15 lb/in) per 1% deviation from 50/50 weight distribution.
categories.concreteNumbers: Frequency reference (Hz to multiplier): 1.21 Hz = 0.10 (Very Soft), 1.98 Hz = 0.20 (Soft), 2.42 Hz = 0.30 (Moderate), 2.80 Hz = 0.40 (Stiff), 3.43 Hz = 0.60 (Heavy aero/limited travel). Example: 2133 lb car, 52% front × 0.2 multiplier = 221.8 lb/in front spring. Racing baselines (percentage sliders): Sports class Front 87-98%, Rear 58-80%; High Performance Front 85-93%, Rear 63-84%; Race Car Front 83-93%, Rear 59-85% (RWD/AWD; FWD reverses).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Road race: higher stiffness (0.40 multiplier, 70-80% slider range). Drift: split tuning Front 50%, Rear 85%. Off-Road: much softer (0.20-0.25 multiplier, 39-40% front / 6-7% rear slider). Drag: moderate stiffness for launch stability.
categories.edgeCases: Rally springs half Race Spring values. Off-Road springs ~40% front / 7% rear slider position. FWD reverses spring front/rear ratios compared to RWD (softer front for steering). Physical constraint: (Front SR × Front RH × 2) + (Rear SR × Rear RH × 2) ≥ Vehicle Weight ensures adequate load support.

categories.formula: Front Bump = Min Bump + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6 (bump is 60% of rebound). Rear Damping Offset based on spring rate difference: 0-1.5% diff Rear Rebound -0.2 / Bump -0.1; 1.5-35% diff -0.3 / -0.2; 36-40% diff -0.6 / -0.4; above 40% diff -1.2 / -0.8. General rule: Rebound should be much higher than bump (1.5x to 1.7x ratio). Rally/Off-Road add +1.0 to all rebound values.
categories.concreteNumbers: Example 1400 kg (60% front), Sports tier: Bump 5.5, Rebound 9.2. Bump stiffness minimum by class: Sports 4.4-4.8, High Performance 4.5-4.9, Race Car 4.7-4.9. Baseline ranges: Circuit Racing Bump Front 5.0 / Rear 4.0, Rebound Front 8.0 / Rear 7.0; Street Racing Bump Front 4.0 / Rear 3.5, Rebound Front 6.0 / Rear 5.5; Drifting Bump Front 2.0 / Rear 1.0, Rebound Front 4.0 / Rear 3.0; Off-Road Bump Front 1.0 / Rear 1.0, Rebound Front 3.0 / Rear 3.0.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Road race: higher damping ratios (bump 5-6, rebound 8-10) for stability. Street/Casual: moderate (bump 4-5, rebound 6-8). Drift: low damping (bump 2-3, rebound 4-5) for tire responsiveness. Off-Road: very low (bump 1-2, rebound 3-4) for compliance.
categories.edgeCases: Bump must be 50-75% of rebound (typical 60%) for proper tire contact response. Rally/off-road adds +1.0 across rebound. Weight distribution heavily impacts front bump calculation. Spring rate stiffness changes require rebalanced damping.

categories.formula: No numerical formula published. Strategy: select slider position 0-10 range based on discipline. Ride height changes affect camber (must be re-tuned). Physical constraint: (Front SR × Front RH × 2) + (Rear SR × Rear RH × 2) ≥ Vehicle Weight.
categories.concreteNumbers: Road race: minimum both ends. Dirt rally: 70-80% of slider range. Cross-country: maximum both ends. Drag: minimum front, slightly higher rear. Drift: minimum both ends. General race car ranges: 4-6 (low aero focus), Street cars: 5-7, Prototypes: 3.5-4.5, Off-roaders: 5-7.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Drag: minimize RH for aerodynamic efficiency and load transfer. Rally/Off-Road: raise RH (70-80%) for ground clearance and compliance. Cross-country: maximum RH for obstacle clearance. Drift: minimize for weight transfer control.
categories.edgeCases: Ride height changes trigger automatic camber recalculation—always re-check alignment after RH adjustments. Low RH improves aero but reduces suspension travel; high RH improves ground clearance but increases aero drag and CG height.

categories.formula: See damping entry above (rebound/bump calculations).
categories.concreteNumbers: See damping concrete numbers above.
categories.goalVariation: See damping goal variation above.
categories.edgeCases: See damping edge cases above.

categories.formula: No explicit drag coefficient or downforce formula published. Conceptual rule: higher front/rear downforce slider position = more aero load = more drag = lower top speed. Balance point: set overall downforce until Aero Balance = 0.40-0.45 (higher = more front downforce). Efficiency metric: Downforce / Drag = Aero Efficiency (target 0.70-0.85 normal range).
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance targets: Road racing AWD 0.40-0.45, Road racing RWD 0.50-0.55, neutral 0.50. Aero Efficiency ranges: above 0.85 Excellent, 0.70-0.85 Average, below 0.70 Poor. Front/Rear strategy: AWD Front Max / Rear Min; RWD Front 60-80% / Rear 60-90%; FWD Front High-Max / Rear Low-Moderate. Unit conversion: 1 KGF ≈ 2.2 lb.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Time-attack: maximize downforce (both sliders high), Aero Balance 0.40-0.45. Sprint/Speed-focused: minimize rear downforce, moderate front. Drag: moderate to high front downforce for launch; minimize rear for top speed. Off-Road: typically aero disabled or minimal.
categories.edgeCases: Aero sliders interact with drag—each unit of front/rear downforce increases drag. Run both front AND rear aero or neither (running one without other creates high-speed imbalance). Drag racing needs front downforce for launch stability but minimized rear for top speed. Top-heavy aero builds may require suspension stiffening to handle increased downforce loads.

categories.formula: Brake bias percentage represents front/rear pressure split: 50% = equal front/rear; >50% = more front bias (stability); <50% = more rear bias (rotation). Weight transfer principle: during braking, weight shifts forward, so rear tires become lighter (prone to locking first). Therefore bias should be set so front locks slightly before rear. Formula basis: bias should approximate inverse of static weight distribution—heavier rear requires more rear brake bias, heavier front requires more front bias. Specific formula: For cars with known forward weight %, start with approximately (100 - Forward Weight %) / 2 adjusted by ~2-4% based on brake pressure and trail-braking preference.
categories.concreteNumbers: General range 48-55% front bias. Road racing circuit: 52-55% front (stability-first). Trail-braking: 54-58% rear bias (front-engine cars), 55-59% rear bias (mid/rear-engine). Examples: McLaren 12C GT3 (45% forward) = 52% bias, RWD 52% forward = ~48% bias, Mid/rear-engine ~42% forward = ~54% bias. Brake pressure: 100% default; 105-115% aggressive (heavy cars); 85-95% softer (trail-brake light cars). Drift/Dirt: 45-50% (rear-biased).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit/Stability-first: 52-55% front bias. Trail-braking (experienced): 53-59% rear bias (varies by engine position). Drag: stabilize launch (moderate front bias). Drift: rear-biased 45-50% for rotation control. Off-Road: moderate, ~50-52% (depends on terrain and weight).
categories.edgeCases: Mid/rear-engine cars need higher rear brake bias (54-59%) than front-engine (52-55%). Brake pressure settings above 100% require bias adjustment upward. Higher brake pressure may lock rear earlier—adjust bias forward. Trail-braking requires higher bias understanding and precision. ABS enabled: can run lower bias (43-40%) for shortest braking distance but loses trail-braking capability.

categories.formula: No universal formula; percentage-based adjustment ranges depend on drivetrain. Acceleration (throttle): higher % locks wheels together (more traction, higher understeer tendency). Deceleration (lift-off/braking): higher % increases rear coupling for stability. RWD deceleration principle: 1% decrease reduces understeer in 2-3% increments; 1% decrease in acceleration reduces turn-exit understeer. AWD center differential (torque distribution): percentage to rear (higher = more rear power/bias).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD Acceleration 40-65% (road race 40-65%, drift 80-100%); Deceleration 15-30% (road race). FWD Acceleration 25-35% (must avoid below 25%). AWD typical road race: Front Accel 28% / Decel 0%, Rear Accel 100% / Decel 45%, Center Balance 70-85% rear bias. AWD rally: Center Balance 65-75% rear bias. AWD cross-country: Center Balance 55-65% rear bias. Adjustment: reduce accel by 2-3% to decrease exit understeer; reduce decel by 1% to decrease entry understeer.
categories.goalVariation: Road race RWD: 50-55% accel / 15-30% decel. Drift RWD: 80-100% accel / higher decel for slide control. Road race AWD: 70-85% center rear bias. Rally AWD: 65-75% center rear bias. Cross-country AWD: 55-65% center rear bias. FWD: only adjust front diff (25-35% accel range). Drag RWD: higher accel % (55-65%) for launch traction.
categories.edgeCases: RWD above 75% accel risks severe understeer and poor turn-in. FWD below 25% accel causes loss of grip (wheelspin). AWD center differential heavily influences feel—rear bias (70-85%) for sport/neutral, front bias (30-40%) for stability. Power delivery and suspension setup significantly affect optimal diff settings (no universal formula). Trail-braking sensitivity: higher decel % reduces lift-off oversteer but increases on-throttle understeer.

source: https://www.forzafire.com/guides/forza-horizon-6-platform-and-handling-tuning-guide (primary formula source); https://forzatune.com/guide/the-fully-updated-forza-tuning-guide/ (mechanical balance, tuning order); https://apexspeedcraft.wordpress.com/2026/04/25/forza-horizon-tuning-reference/ (AWD meta baseline, comprehensive settings)
reliability: High for alignment, springs damping, ARBs, and mechanical balance; Medium for differential and gearing (no proprietary formulas disclosed); Aero balance targets are present but formula is not disclosed

categories.formula: Camber (negative degrees) = discipline-dependent range. Caster range = 5.0-7.0 degrees. Toe default = 0.0 degrees (minor adjustments only 0.1-0.3 degrees).
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: camber -1.0 to -2.0 front / -0.5 to -1.0 rear; Drift: camber -3.0 to -5.0 front / -1.0 rear, toe +1.0 front out / -0.1 to -0.2 rear in; Rally: camber -0.8 to -1.2 front / -0.5 to -0.8 rear, toe +0.1 front out; Off-road: camber -0.5 front/rear; Cross-country: camber -0.5 front/rear
categories.concreteNumbers: Caster: default 5.0 degrees, recommended 6.0-7.0 degrees for road racing; Camber typical road racing: -1.5 to -2.5 front, -1.0 to -2.0 rear; AWD meta baseline: -1.8 front / -1.2 rear; Toe: 0.0 neutral (no scrub)
categories.edgeCases: Light/agile cars use lower caster (5.0-5.5°); heavy/high-speed cars use higher caster (6.5-7.0°). FWD may tolerate different camber than RWD due to weight transfer. Drift builds use extreme front camber (-5.0°). No thermal modeling in Forza; cold and hot camber behave identically.

categories.formula: Rate = ((Max Rate - Min Rate) × Slider Position %) + Min Rate. Weight distribution adjustment: Shift 4 kgf/mm (or 15 lb/in) per 1% deviation from 50/50. Example: If front is 2% lighter, subtract 8 kgf/mm from front springs and add 8 kgf/mm to rear.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: stiff baseline; Drag: very stiff (maximize transfer control); Drift: moderate to stiff; Rally/Off-road: softer (65-75% of max height for suspension travel); Cross-country: maximum height for articulation
categories.concreteNumbers: Absolute values are car-specific (shown in tune menu, min/max per vehicle). Tune by ratio instead of absolute. AWD road baseline example: 1,400 kg car at 60% front weight uses proportional front-to-rear stiffness. Off-road/rally: 65-75% max height.
inputs: weight distribution percentage (% on front axle)
categories.edgeCases: Forza spring minima are well above real-world use. Front-to-rear ratio matters more than absolute values. FWD and RWD need different front/rear balance due to weight transfer characteristics. AWD changes weight distribution under power, affecting optimal spring ratio.

categories.formula: Front Bump = Min Bump (class tier) + (Front Weight in lb / 200) × 0.1. Front Rebound = Front Bump / 0.6. Core ratio: rebound ≈ 1.5x to 1.8x bump damping. Rear applies offset adjustments based on spring rate difference: 0-1.5% diff = -0.2 rebound / -0.1 bump; 1.5-35% = -0.3 / -0.2; 36-40% = -0.6 / -0.4; above 40% = -1.2 / -0.8. (FWD setups with stiffer rear use negative offsets.)
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: balanced rebound ratio (1.5x bump); Drift: adjusted for mid-drift stability; Drag: slightly higher bump for launch; Rally/Off-road: may differ based on terrain and spring choice
categories.concreteNumbers: AWD road baseline: 5.4 bump / 13.5 rebound front; 5.2 / 13.0 rear. Sports tier min bump: 4.4-4.8. High Performance: 4.5-4.9. Race Car: 4.7-4.9. Starting point example (rebound 18.0 / bump 6.0) applies as a general reference.
categories.edgeCases: Rebound significantly higher than bump is critical for platform stability. FWD (front-heavy) and RWD (rear-heavy) require different bump/rebound ratios. Large spring rate differences between axles trigger larger offset adjustments. AWD center diff effects on damping behavior not explicitly disclosed.

categories.formula: Base = (Weight in lb / 2) / (200 - 200 × Stiffness%). Distribution by drivetrain: RWD +1 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%; FWD -1 ARB per 1% (front becomes soft); AWD +0.66 ARB per 1% front weight above 50%. Example (1400 kg AWD car, 60% front, High Performance 43% stiffness): Base = (3,087 / 2) / (200 - 86) = 13.5. AWD adjustment +0.66 × 10 = +6.6 front, -6.6 rear. Front ARB: 20.1 | Rear ARB: 6.9
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road): target mechanical balance 0.55-0.65 (sweet spot 0.60); Drift: variable (stiffer rear for oversteer); Drag: may use extreme settings; Off-road/Rally: softer overall
categories.concreteNumbers: Stiffer front = understeer. Stiffer rear = oversteer. AWD meta exploit: 1.0 front / 65.0 rear (extreme, dominates competitive). Road baseline: 1-65 or 12-22 (rally). Off-road: 10-12. Scale 1-65.
inputs: car class stiffness % (Sports 61-65, High Perf 40-46, Race 35-62)
categories.edgeCases: AWD uses +0.66x multiplier (softer adjustment than RWD). FWD front becomes very soft due to weight. ARBs are the primary tool for hitting mechanical balance 0.55-0.65. Extreme ARB ratios (1/65 AWD) create exploitative handling but dominate meta.

categories.formula: No formula. Slider-based selection per track type requirement.
categories.goalVariation: Road racing: minimum both ends (cornering clearance). Drag: minimum front, slightly higher rear (rake forward, weight transfer). Dirt rally: 70-80% of slider range (suspension travel). Cross-country: maximum both ends (articulation). Drift: variable.
categories.concreteNumbers: Minimum = 0% slider. Maximum = 100% slider. Off-road tracks require 70-80% to avoid bottoming. Road requires minimum for aerodynamic efficiency and cornering.
categories.edgeCases: Lower height improves aero but risks bottoming on bumps. Higher height provides articulation on rough surfaces. No numerical measurement unit disclosed; slider percentage only.

categories.formula: No formula for compound selection. Pressure guidelines: Stock/Street/Rally = 26-28 psi front/rear; Semi-slick/Slick/Drift = 30-33 psi front/rear; Race Slick 28.0-32.5 psi; Off-Road 15.5-21.0 psi.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: slick tires, 30-33 psi (heat-stable). Drag: soft compound for launch, 55 psi front / 15 psi rear (minimum setup). Drift: semi-slick or slick, 30-33 psi. Rally: rally compound, 24-26 psi. Off-road: off-road tire, 15.5-21 psi.
categories.concreteNumbers: No thermal modeling in Forza; cold and hot pressure behave identically. Target drag setup: 55 front / 15 rear (minimum pressure). Road racing general: 32-34 psi hot (regardless of real physics modeling).
categories.edgeCases: Drag builds use extreme pressure imbalance (55f/15r) for weight transfer. Off-road uses lower pressures. No tire wear or degradation in Forza; pressure is a handling tool only.

categories.formula: Brake Balance ranges 50% (equal) to higher (front-biased). Brake Pressure ranges 70% to 200% (100% default balanced). No explicit formula for pressure-balance interaction.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit (road, high-speed): 51-53% front balance. RWD road: 50-55% front. AWD road: 52-56% front. FWD road: 55-62% front. Drift/Dirt: 45-50% (rear-biased). Drag: varies.
categories.concreteNumbers: Balance: 50% = equal. RWD typical: 50-55% front. AWD typical: 52-56% front. FWD typical: 55-62% front. Pressure: 100% default; above 100% increases stopping force; below 100% reduces for trail-braking finesse. Never below 48% front balance.
categories.edgeCases: RWD needs balanced to slightly front-biased (weight transfer benefit). AWD needs more front bias (center diff fights rotation). FWD needs heavy front bias (no rear braking help). Drift uses rear-biased (stability in slide). Pressure above 100% without ABS risks locking rear wheels.

categories.formula: No disclosed formula. Acceleration (%) controls locking under throttle: higher = wheels forced to similar speeds. Deceleration (%) controls locking off-throttle/under braking: higher = rear-wheel coupling. Center Diff (AWD only, %) controls front-rear torque split: typically 70-85% toward rear for road.
categories.goalVariation: RWD road: Accel 40-65%, Decel 15-30%. RWD drift: Accel 80-100% (near-welded). AWD road: Accel 45% front / 65% rear, Decel 0% front / 15% rear, Center Diff 70-85% rear bias. FWD: Accel 40-60% (varies by platform).
categories.concreteNumbers: RWD typical road: 60% accel / 20% decel. AWD typical road: 45% accel front / 65% accel rear, 0% decel front / 15% decel rear. Center Diff: 70-75% neutral road, 80-85% off-road. Drift (all drivetrain): 100% accel / 100% decel (fully locked).
categories.edgeCases: RWD too high accel = understeer. Too low accel = wheelspin. Too high decel = lift-off understeer; too low = lift-off oversteer. AWD center diff 70-85% toward rear ensures rear bias in road racing (front-heavy AWD). Drift requires max lock (100/100) for predictability.

categories.formula: No official formula disclosed. Community standard: space gears logarithmically (wider gaps at bottom, tighter at top). Final Drive is a uniform multiplier applied to all gears: lower FD = taller gears (higher top speed, slower accel); higher FD = shorter gears (lower top speed, better accel). Calculators use power-to-weight ratio to suggest FD but don't publish the calculation.
categories.goalVariation: Circuit: FD tuned for optimal power band usage without bouncing rev limiter. Drag: short gears for low-end launch power, high FD. Drift: medium-short for throttle control. Rally/Off-road: variable per terrain and car.
categories.concreteNumbers: Final Drive has no disclosed numerical formula. Individual gear spacing: progressive logarithmic curve. More spread 1st-to-2nd (larger acceleration difference); tighter spacing at top end (air resistance grows exponentially). No exact ratios provided in sources.
categories.edgeCases: Drag builds use extreme short gearing with high FD for 0-100 km/h. Circuit tunes balance top speed and power band. AWD distributes torque differently, affecting gear load. Engine type (ICE vs EV) affects power delivery curve but no explicit gearing formula change disclosed.

categories.formula: No disclosed formula. Aero Balance = target value (0.40-0.55 depending on drivetrain). Front and rear downforce adjusted via slider to hit target balance. Relationship: more wing angle = more downforce + more drag (lower top speed).
categories.goalVariation: AWD road racing: Aero Balance 0.40-0.45 (front-biased, front max / rear min). RWD road racing: 0.50-0.55 (neutral to rear-biased). FWD road racing: 0.45-0.55 (slightly front-biased). Drift: 0.45-0.50. Circuit tight: maximize downforce. Top-speed tracks: minimize downforce.
categories.concreteNumbers: Aero Balance neutral = 0.50. AWD target: 0.40-0.45 (front biased). RWD target: 0.50-0.55. FWD target: 0.45-0.55. Aero Efficiency benchmark: Above 0.85 = Excellent, 0.70-0.85 = Average, Below 0.70 = Poor. Unit conversion: 1 kgf ≈ 2.2 lb downforce.
categories.edgeCases: AWD typically runs front max / rear min (extreme aero imbalance due to stability needs). RWD neutral-to-rear biased. Only effective at speed (no benefit at low speeds). No disclosed mathematical relationship between slider position and downforce magnitude.

categories.formula: Tuning sequence order (applied sequentially): Tires → Springs → Ride Height → Alignment → ARBs → Damping → Brakes → Differential → Aero → Gearing. Each adjustment cascades, so order matters.
categories.goalVariation: Order is universal across all disciplines but the values plugged into each step vary by goal (circuit/drift/drag/rally/off-road).
categories.concreteNumbers: This is a sequence framework, not numerical values. Apply in this exact order for reproducible results.
categories.edgeCases: Skipping order or tuning in parallel reduces visibility into which change caused the effect. This sequence ensures methodical tuning and reproducibility.
